1100710700.english: Taylor! Didn't you write an adult novel with the commander as the protagonist without permission!
1100710700.korean: 테일러! 자네는 허가도 받지 않고 사령관이 주인공인 성인 소설을 쓰지 않았나!

1100710701.english: Even Upohwa! Alpha saw the novel you wrote...
1100710701.korean: 심지어 유포까지! 알파가 자네가 쓴 소설을 보고…

1100710703.english: I don't know what kind of excitement you're talking about, but it's probably not the excitement you're thinking of...
1100710703.korean: 자네가 말하는 흥분이 뭔지는 모르겠지만 자네가 생각하는 그 흥분은 아닐 걸세…

1100710705.english: No matter how much you rant to me, I belong to the executive branch, not the legislative branch…
1100710705.korean: 백날 나에게 떠들어도 나는 입법부가 아니라 행정부 소속이라네…

1100710706.english: And Elven Forest Maker! Didn't you add unauthorized additives to the Elven Milk!
1100710706.korean: 그리고 엘븐 포레스트 메이커! 자네는 엘븐 밀크에 허가 받지 않은 첨가물을 넣지 않았나!

1100710707.english: That is a violation of the Food Sanitation Act!

1100710708.english: No, who are you saying Elven Milk tastes bad! I put it in with good intentions, hoping you would drink a lot of Elven Milk! I had absolutely no bad intentions!
1100710708.korean: 아니, 누가 엘븐 밀크가 맛 없다고 까잖아! 엘븐 밀크를 많이 먹기를 바라는 선의로 넣은 거라고! 나쁜 마음은 전혀 없었어!

1100710709.english: There are members who are allergic to those additives! And didn't you leave out the labeling!
1100710709.korean: 그 첨가물 중에 알레르기가 있는 대원도 있단 말일세! 그리고 표기를 빼먹지 않았나!

1100710710.english: Aurora barely managed to stop Sowon from slaughtering you because the result of cooking the Commander with the Elven Milk you processed was unsatisfactory!
1100710710.korean: 소완이 자네가 가공한 엘븐 밀크로 사령관이 먹을 요리를 했다가 결과물이 시원찮으니까 자네를 도륙내버리겠다고 하는 걸 아우로라가 간신히 막았단 말일세!

1100710712.english: Didn't you fight with other members in the community, accumulate reports, and even commit defamation!
1100710712.korean: 자네는 다른 대원들과 커뮤니티에서 싸워서 신고가 누적되고 명예훼손죄까지 범하지 않았나!

1100710713.english: Is telling the truth a crime?

1100710714.english: If you go around saying that Colonel Knight Angel has a flat chest, wouldn't that be defamation, even if it were true?
1100710714.korean: 나이트 앤젤 대령의 가슴은 납작하다고 말하고 다니면 아무리 사실이라고 하더라도 명예훼손이 되지 않겠나.

1100710715.english: Then, if you say, "Colonel Knight Angel has big breasts. That's common sense," is there no problem at all?
1100710715.korean: 그러면 ‘나이트 앤젤 대령의 가슴은 크다. 그건 상식이잖아.’라고 말하면 아무런 문제도 없습니까?

1100710716.english: … … Even if a bomb suddenly falls on your head one day, just think, "Ah, that day has come."
1100710716.korean: ……어느 날 갑자기 머리에 폭탄이 떨어져도 아, 그날이 왔구나 하게나.

1100710718.english: strictly distinguish between online and offline.

1100710721.english: I will exercise my right to remain silent.

1100710722.english: So it's you... the one who trashed Elven Milk saying it tastes bad.
1100710722.korean: 너구나… 엘븐 밀크 맛 없다고 깐 놈이.

1100710732.english: I just wanted everyone in Orca to eat their fill!
1100710732.korean: 나는 오르카의 모두가 배불리 먹기를 바랐을 뿐이라고!

1100710734.english: Oh~ To think you hit a deer's head from that far away with a pistol. That's impressive.
1100710734.korean: 오~ 저렇게 멀리 떨어진 사슴 머리를 권총으로 명중시키다니. 대단한데?

1100710735.english: Praise me more. Everyone’s known about this Werewolf's marksmanship for a long time.
1100710735.korean: 더 칭찬하라고. 이 워울프 님의 총솜씨는 옛날부터 알아줬으니까.

1100710738.english: Deer are part of nature, too. As a member of the Watcher of Nature, shouldn't you be stopping the deer hunting?
1100710738.korean: 사슴도 자연의 일부잖아. 와쳐 오브 네이쳐 소속으로서 사슴 사냥을 말려야하는 거 아냐?

1100710739.english: Excluding humans from nature is not the philosophy of the Watcher of Nature.
1100710739.korean: 자연에서 사람을 제외하는 건 와쳐 오브 네이쳐의 사상이 아니야.

1100710740.english: The basic philosophy of Watcher of Nature is that humans can coexist with nature.
1100710740.korean: 와쳐 오브 네이쳐는 인간도 자연과 공존할 수 있다는 것이 기본 사상이야.

1100710741.english: In the era when the Watcher of Nature was emerging, humans had no choice but to utilize nature to maintain their way of life.
1100710741.korean: 와쳐 오브 네이쳐가 태동하던 시대에는 인간이 당시의 생활을 유지하기 위해서는 자연을 이용하는 것은 어쩔 수 없었지.

1100710742.english: Of course, the biggest reason was that if they engaged in radical environmental activities, it was obvious they would be blacklisted by corporations and the state and disbanded.
1100710742.korean: 물론 가장 큰 이유는 과격한 생태주의 활동을 펼쳤으면 기업과 국가에 찍혀서 해산될 게 뻔하다는 이유였지만.

1100710743.english: And in this era, nature has been greatly restored thanks to the extinction of humans.
1100710743.korean: 그리고 지금 시대는 인간이 멸종한 덕분에 자연이 많이 복원됐잖아.

1100710744.english: If you only catch as much as you need, there is no big problem-

1100710747.english: Salamander! Hurry up and gather it up! It's all washing away!

1100710748.english: I'm not sure if this is right...

1100710750.english: Blast fishing… the fish are one thing, but…

1100710751.english: Hey, you brats! Who told you to go bomb fishing!
1100710751.korean: 야이, 자식들아! 어느 누가 폭파 낚시하래!

1100710752.english: The biological environment is going to be completely destroyed!

1100710753.english: Do you want to get smashed to pieces by me!

1100710754.english: Never mind that, the Iron Bugs are swarming in because of the sound!
1100710754.korean: 그건 둘째치고 소리 듣고 철충 몰려오잖아!

1100710756.english: Let's unleash it to our heart's content!

1100710757.english: I just caught the fish we needed for the party!

1100710758.english: If you're not happy with that, don't eat the fish I caught!
1100710758.korean: 그게 불만이면 내가 잡은 물고기 먹지마!

1100710759.english: Tsk tsk. They say there's no grave without an excuse and no criminal who doesn't feel wronged.
1100710759.korean: 쯧쯧. 세상에 핑계 없는 무덤은 없고, 안 억울한 범죄자는 없다더니.

1100710760.english: That's a fine thing to do.

1100710761.english: Huh? Come to think of it, Pupp Head. Can you turn on the radio now?
1100710761.korean: 응? 그러고보니 펍 헤드. 지금 라디오 틀어줄 수 있어?

1100710763.english: You're a criminal, and you're asking for a lot! No way!
1100710763.korean: 범죄자 주제에 바라는 것도 많군! 안 되-

1100710764.english: No? What is this? It's a battery, right? Pupp Head, I found a battery that doesn't belong to anyone. Should I turn it in to the police?
1100710764.korean: 아니? 이게 뭐지? 전지잖아? 펍 헤드, 주인 없는 전지를 주웠는데 이거 경찰에게 맡겨야겠지?

1100710765.english: It's not going to work, it's just not going to work. It's truly a shame that someone with such a strong sense of lawfulness like you is in a place like this.
1100710765.korean: 안 될 일이지, 안 될 일이야. 준법 정신이 뛰어난 자네가 이런 곳에 있다니 참으로 안 될 일일세.

1100710766.english: But I'm just a powerless police officer. All I can do is make things convenient for you until you leave here.
1100710766.korean: 하지만 나는 힘 없는 경찰. 내가 할 수 있는 것은 자네가 여기에서 나갈 때까지 편의를 봐주는 것밖에 할 수 없다네.

1100710767.english: Ahem! I'll give you the battery. I'll help you find your owner-
1100710767.korean: 어흠! 전지 주게. 내가 주인을 찾아주겠-

1100710771.english: It's a bribery charge. You go in too.

1100710775.english: Taylor. You're also being charged with bribery.

1100710777.english: Still, I'll let you listen to the radio.

1100710778.english: It's the new year, after all.

1100710781.english: Hello, Orca, and listeners in Europe, or rather, around the world.
1100710781.korean: 안녕하세요, 오르카, 유럽, 아니, 전세계의 청취자 여러분.

1100710782.english: Orangeade Radio Orca Live! In short, the DJ of OraOra, the headhunter of Orca, the adjutant and sister machine of Lemonade Alpha (omitted)
1100710782.korean: 오렌지에이드 라디오 오르카 라이브! 줄여서 오라오라의 DJ이자 오르카의 헤드헌터, 레모네이드 알파 님의 부관이자 자매기(중략)

1100710783.english: (Omitted) Orca Library Assistant Manager! And finally! Finally! It's Orangeade, the social media platform within Orca's internal network, Orca Social, that has been unlocked!
1100710783.korean: (중략)오르카 라이브러리 보조 관리자! 그리고 드디어! 마침내! 오르카 내부망 SNS 소셜 오르카가 해금된 오렌지에이드에요!

1100710784.english: I've been dying to do Social Orca for so long, but I haven't been able to, and I'm so frustrated.
1100710784.korean: 그 동안 소셜 오르카를 하고 싶었는데 할 수 없어서 얼마나 안달이 났는지 몰라요.

1100710785.english: Since some of my work involves confidential information, Lemonade Alpha-nim prohibited it.
1100710785.korean: 제가 하는 업무 중에 기밀과 관련된 일이 있다보니 레모네이드 알파 님께서 금지시켰거든요.

1100710786.english: Oh, and that's not to say Lemonade Alpha is bad. Considering Alpha-nim's position and my role, it's a natural course of action.
1100710786.korean: 아, 그렇다고 레모네이드 알파 님이 나쁘다는 건 아니에요. 알파 님의 입장과 저의 역할을 생각하면 당연한 조치에요.

1100710787.english: So, no badmouthing Alpha! It's prohibited because it's a measure that the parties involved have agreed upon. Anyway, it's forbidden!
1100710787.korean: 그러니까 알파 님 욕하는 건 금지! 당사자끼리 납득한 조치니까 금지! 아무튼 금지!

1100710788.english: I'm definitely not saying this because Alpha-nim is watching from the control room! It's true! Really! Really! Without a shred of falsehood! Truly! We get along well! Because we're sisters, both boss and subordinate! I love you, big sister!
1100710788.korean: 결코 조정실에서 알파 님께서 지켜보고 계셔서 이런 말하는 건 아니에요! 진짜에요! 정말로! 참말로! 일말의 거짓도 없이! 진실로! 저희 사이 좋아요! 상사와 부하인 동시에 자매기니까! 사랑해요, 언니!

1100710789.english: That's not the important thing! The important thing is! Me! Orangeade! Finally! Finally! So you created a Social Orca account! To be precise, it's the official SNS of the Ora Ora DJ Orangeade!
1100710789.korean: 중요한 건 그게 아니죠! 중요한 건! 제가! 오렌지에이드가! 마침내! 드디어! 소셜 오르카 계정을 만들었다는 거죠! 정확하게 말하자면 오라오라 DJ 오렌지에이드의 공식 SNS죠!

1100710790.english: You can find my official SNS by searching for "OraOra" or "Orangeade" on Social Orca! Beware of impersonation accounts! Please check the official Orca mark next to the name!
1100710790.korean: 소셜 오르카에서 오라오라 혹은 오렌지에이드로 검색하시면 저의 공식 SNS를 찾으실 수 있어요! 사칭 계정 조심! 이름 옆에 있는 오르카 공식마크를 확인해주세요!

1100710791.english: As the DJ for OraOra, I'll be posting a lot, so please follow me!
1100710791.korean: 오라오라의 DJ로서 많은 글을 올릴 테니 많은 팔로우 부탁드려요!

1100710792.english: The goal is to break into the top 5 in the Social Orca follower rankings! If possible, achieve 2nd place!
1100710792.korean: 목표는 소셜 오르카 팔로워 순위 5위권 진입! 가능하면 2위 달성!

1100710793.english: The commander is in first place, of course, hehe. This is untouchable.
1100710793.korean: 1위는 사령관님이시니까요, 헤헤. 이건 넘볼 수 없죠.

1100710794.english: And in the meantime, a new post has been updated!
1100710794.korean: 그리고 그 사이에 새 글 하나 갱신!

1100710795.english: Hehe, here you go! Then let's end the personal talk here and get started in earnest! We're starting our special New Year's broadcast!
1100710795.korean: 헤헤, 자! 그러면 사적인 이야기는 여기서 끝내고 본격적으로! 오라오라 새해 기념 특별 편성 방송을 시작합니다!

1100710796.english: Happy New Year, everyone. Well, it's a bit late to say "Happy New Year," but since it's still January, it's still the new year.
1100710796.korean: 새해가 밝았습니다, 여러분. 뭐, 새해라고 말하기에는 조금 늦은 감이 없잖아 있지만 아직 1월달이니까 새해는 새해죠.

1100710797.english: Hearing "New Year" reminds me of a few years ago when I was sent on a special mission by Lemonade Alpha-nim to New Zealand for work during the New Year.
1100710797.korean: 새해라고 하니까 몇 년 전 새해에 레모네이드 알파 님의 특명을 받고 뉴질랜드로 외근을 나간 일이 떠오르네요.

1100710798.english: I've done a lot of fieldwork, but there's a specific reason why that particular fieldwork stands out in my memory. I don't remember the easy and comfortable field trips very well, but the difficult ones stay with me for a long time.
1100710798.korean: 제가 많은 외근을 수행했는데 유달리 그 외근이 기억나는 이유가 따로 있어요. 쉽고 편했던 외근은 기억이 잘 안나지만 고생한 기억은 오래 남잖아요.

1100710799.english: You know, that mission went wrong from the very beginning. Finding a ferry to New Zealand was the first challenge. It was hard to find even the ships, and most of them were just coastal vessels. The ship I finally found going to the South Pacific wasn't headed to New Zealand either.
1100710799.korean: 그 임무가 시작부터 삐걱거렸던 거 있죠. 뉴질랜드로 가는 배편을 찾는 것부터 일이었죠. 배부터 찾기 힘들었는데 대부분의 배가 연안에서만 오가는 배였죠. 간신히 찾은 원양으로 가는 배도 목적지가 뉴질랜드가 아니었고요.

1100710800.english: But who am I? I'm Orangeade, the special headhunter who succeeds in any situation. Oh, orangeade, orangeade, why are you so capable that you're making things difficult for yourself? If I weren't so capable, I wouldn't have had to go thru so much hardship.
1100710800.korean: 하지만 제가 누군가요! 어떤 상황에서도 임무를 성공시키는 스페셜 헤드헌터가 바로 저 오렌지에이드죠. 어휴, 오렌지에이드야, 오렌지에이드야 너는 왜 이리 유능해서 사서 고생이니? 제가 유능하지만 않았으면 그렇게 고생도 안 했을 텐데.

1100710801.english: After searching high and low, I finally found a ship to New Zealand and managed to hitch a ride. No, but that ship turned out to be the traffickers' ship, didn't it!? Well, since we're androids, it's Bioroid trafficking, not human trafficking.
1100710801.korean: 발품을 팔고 팔아서 간신히 뉴질랜드 행 배편을 찾아서 얻어탔죠. 아니, 그런데 그 배가 인신매매범들의 배였던 거 있죠!? 뭐, 우리는 바이오로이드니까 human trafficking이 아니라 bioroid trafficking이긴 하지만요.

1100710802.english: On a ship with nowhere to run, the human traffickers' clutches are reaching for that orangeade (excerpt).
1100710802.korean: 도망칠 곳 없는 배 위에서 인신매매범들의 마수가 저 오렌지에이드에게 향하는데(중략)

1100710803.english: (Omitted) As a result, I befriended the human traffickers and reformed them! That's the friendliness of my orangeade! You can become friends with anyone, no matter who they are! So, I was safely heading to New Zealand. No, suddenly pirates there! (Omitted)
1100710803.korean: (중략)결과적으로 인신매매범들과 친해져서 개과천선시켰죠! 이게 바로 저 오렌지에이드의 친화력! 상대가 그 누구라도 친해질 수 있다! 그래서 무사히 뉴질랜드로 향하고 있었는데. 아니, 거기서 갑자기 해적이!(중략)

1100710804.english: (Omitted) After this dynamic chase, they blew up the pirates' hideout! The pirates, having nowhere to go, were taken aboard the ships of human traffickers, and together they searched for a place to settle down. I barely made it to a port town in Africa, and... It turns out this village is ruled by an oppressor android! (Omitted)
1100710804.korean: (중략)이 다이나믹한 추격전 끝에 해적의 본거지를 폭파! 갈 곳 잃은 해적들을 전 인신매매범들의 배에 태워서 함께 안주할 땅을 찾아다녔죠. 간신히 아프리카의 항구마을에 도착했는데. 이 마을 알고 보니 압제자 바이오로이드가 지배하고 있는 마을!(중략)

1100710805.english: (Omitted) Their life-or-death final gamble had succeeded, liberating the civilian Bioroids from oppression and allowing them to build a good village together... but alas! The mission entrusted by Lemonade Alpha! I said tearful goodbyes to the friends I had made and boarded a new ship, heading for New Zealand.
1100710805.korean: (중략)목숨을 건 최후의 도박이 성공하여 민간 바이오로이드들을 압제에서 해방시키고 함께 좋은 마을을 만들고 있었는데… 아뿔싸! 레모네이드 알파 님께서 맡기신 임무! 친해진 사람들과 눈물의 이별을 하고 새로운 배를 타고 뉴질랜드로 향했죠.

1100710806.english: But there was a typhoon on the way! No, why are you doing this to me? Honestly, it felt like this mission was cursed, and the world was trying to stop me from going to New Zealand. I changed course to avoid the typhoon, but it followed me all the way! And eventually, swept up in the typhoon, I, a passenger, was running all over the ship to keep it from sinking. (Omitted)
1100710806.korean: 그런데 가는 길에 태풍이! 아니, 저한테 왜 그러세요? 진짜 이 임무는 무슨 액이 꼈는지 세상이 저를 뉴질랜드로 향하는 것을 막는 거 같았다니까요. 태풍을 피하기 위해 항로를 수정했는데 태풍이 끝까지 쫓아온 거 있죠? 그리고 결국에는 태풍에 휩쓸려서 승객이었던 저도 배가 침몰하지 않게 이리저리 배 안을 뛰어다녔죠.(중략)

1100710807.english: (Omitted) And finally, we escaped the typhoon, but the ship was literally cut in half and on the verge of sinking. Oh! So, Orangeade's life, which was filled with constant fieldwork, ends here! When I was lamenting like that... someone shouted! Land!
1100710807.korean: (중략)그리고 마침내 태풍에서 벗어났지만 배는 말 그대로 반쪽이 되어버려서 침몰하기 직전이었죠. 아! 외근으로 점칠 된 삶을 살았던 오렌지에이드의 삶은 여기서 디 엔드하는구나! 라고 한탄할 때… 누군가 외쳤죠! 육지다!

1100710808.english: That shout jolted me awake too, and when I looked far away... wow! The mountain peaks were just so beautiful. However, the ship is slowly sinking. Although we can't reach land in a boat that's been split in half... the sailors who shared life and death with me in the typhoon are veteran sailors! Working together (omitted)
1100710808.korean: 그 외침에 저도 눈이 번쩍 떠져서 먼 곳을 바라보니… 와! 산봉우리가 그렇게 예쁠 수가 없었죠. 하지만 배는 서서히 침몰하고 있는 상황. 반쪽이 난 배로는 육지까지 갈 수가 없겠지만… 저와 함께 태풍 속에서 생사고락을 함께했던 선원들은 베테랑 선원들! 함께 힘을 합쳐(중략)

1100710809.english: (Omitted) I barely managed to land on the mainland and, after avoiding the Metal Parasites, went to a nearby village to ask where I was. And then... what? Brazil? (Omitted)
1100710809.korean: (중략)간신히 육지에 상륙하고 철충들을 피해서 가까운 마을에 가서 이곳이 어디냐고 물어보니… 네? 브라질요?(중략)

1100710810.english: (Omitted) And! Finally! Arrived in New Zealand, our destination! Now I just have to complete Lemonade Alpha's mission... I thought... Huh? The bikeroid bioweapons? Um... shouldn't that be coming out of the next neighborhood over? (Omitted)
1100710810.korean: (중략)그리고! 마침내! 목적지였던 뉴질랜드에 도착! 이제는 레모네이드 알파 님의 임무만 수행하면 되겠구나… 싶었는데… 네? 폭주족 바이오로이드들요? 어… 그거 옆 동네에서 나와야 하는 거 아닌가요?(중략)

1100710811.english: (Omitted) Really! Really! Really! Finally, we arrived at the farm, our destination! And when I said I came on Lemonade-san's mission, they gave me a box. And what are those contents?
1100710811.korean: (중략)진짜! 진짜! 진짜로! 마침내 목적지였던 농장에 도착! 그리고 레모네이드 님의 임무를 받아서 왔다고 하니까 상자 하나를 줬죠. 그 내용물이란!

1100710816.english: ...What were those hardships, I wonder? Thinking about all the trouble I went thru to get those kiwis, I just got so angry! I was so angry that I ate so much kiwi that my orangeade turned into kiwiade.
1100710816.korean: ……뭐였을까요, 그 고난들? 키위를 받으러 그 생고생을 한 걸 생각하니 열불이 확 난 거 있죠? 열 받아서 오렌지에이드가 키위에이드가 될 정도로 키위로 먹방을 찍었죠.

1100710817.english: It was delicious! Awesome! It wasn't worth my life, but it was purely delicious! I did it moderately, but thinking about the hardship and what I ate, I felt resentful! But...
1100710817.korean: 맛은 있었어요! 엄청! 목숨값이 아니라 순수하게 맛있었어요! 적당히 하지만 그 고생을 하고 먹은 것을 생각하면 억울했죠! 그런데…

1100710818.english: Turns out, even humans before the destruction of those kiwis found them difficult to eat. It was an organic kiwi that even the upper class could only eat occasionally... I heard that before the apocalypse, it cost the same as a diamond of the same weight...
1100710818.korean: 알고 보니 그 키위들 멸망 전의 인간님들도 먹기 힘든. 상류층들도 어쩌다가 먹을 수 있는 유기농 키위였어요… 멸망 전에는 같은 무게의 다이아몬드와 맞먹는 금액이었다고 하더라고요…

1100710819.english: In the end, I had to pay for what I ate with my body. Oh... I didn't realize farming was so hard. Moreover, because of their high-end strategy, they hardly use any machinery and farm entirely by hand, so my beautiful skin was also severely damaged. Hmph! Oh! But suddenly, a crop thief appeared! (Omitted)
1100710819.korean: 결국에는 제가 먹은 만큼 몸으로 지불해야했죠. 아아… 농사가 그렇게 힘든 줄 몰랐어요. 더군다나 고급화 전략으로 인해서 기계는 거의 안 쓰고 인력만으로만 농사를 짓는 곳이라 제 곱디고운 피부도 많이 상했던 거 있죠. 힝! 앗! 그런데 갑자기 농작물 도둑이 나타났다!(중략)

1100710820.english: (Omitted) Having earned merit for protecting the kiwis, the farm owner forgave all the remaining debt, and she barely managed to return to Lemonade Alpha...
1100710820.korean: (중략)키위를 지켜낸 공훈을 세워서 농장주가 남은 빚을 전부 탕감해줘서 간신히 레모네이드 알파 님께 돌아가니…

1100710821.english: ...It wasn't that farm. It turned out to be a farm in the Lemonade Delta. At first, I only told the farmer "Lemonade-san," so that's how she understood it. Well... in the end, you did eat Delta's kiwi and really get on that lady's nerves, so is that alright? Lemonade Delta! Your kiwi was amazing!
1100710821.korean: ……그 농장이 아니었어요. 레모네이드 델타의 농장이었더라구요. 처음에 농장주한테 레모네이드 님이라고만 말해서 그렇게 알아들었던 거였죠. 뭐… 결과적으로는 델타의 키위를 축내고 그 아줌마 속을 박박 긁어 놓은 거니 올라잇? 레모네이드 델타! 네 키위 쩔더라!

1100710822.english: This time, thanks to the flight arranged by Lemonade Alpha, we were able to go to New Zealand and successfully contact our target. This is confidential information, so I'll stop here.
1100710822.korean: 이번에는 레모네이드 알파 님께서 마련해주신 비행편으로 뉴질랜드에 가서 무사히 목표와 컨택할 수 있었죠. 이 이상은 대외비이므로 이야기는 여기까지.

1100710823.english: Thinking back now, that kiwi was really delicious. A kiwi that's ripe enough to be soft and yielding. The skin was tough, but thanks to that, I could peel it off with my fingernails. Deep blue flesh that shines with juice when peeled. One bite and you'll experience a rich sweetness and a pleasant tang. Even the seeds are savory and crispy... Ah! I want to eat it again!
1100710823.korean: 지금 생각해보니 그 키위 정말 맛있었어요. 적당히 익어서 말랑말랑한 키위. 껍질은 질겼지만 덕분에 손톱으로 껍질을 벗길 수 있었죠. 껍질을 벗기면 과즙으로 빛나는 진한 푸른 속살. 한입 베어 물면 진한 단맛과 적당한 신 맛. 씨앗까지 고소하고 바삭바삭하니… 아! 또 먹고 싶다!

1100710824.english: Even thinking about it now, my mouth is watering. It would be delicious just eating it as is, but it would probably be even more delicious made into a parfait.
1100710824.korean: 정말 지금 생각해도 침이 고이네요. 그냥 먹어도 맛있겠지만 파르페로 만들어 먹어도 정말 맛있을 거 같아요.

1100710825.english: In that spirit, let's listen to a song! A song by Manager CS Ferro, the manager of Cafe Amor and a member of the Companion series! Love is like a parfait!
1100710825.korean: 그런 의미로 노래 하나 듣고 가실게요! 컴패니언 시리즈의 일원이자 카페 아모르의 점장CS페로 점장님의 노래! 사랑은 파르페처럼!

1100710826.english: Wow... this person has been talking non-stop for an hour and doesn't seem tired at all.
1100710826.korean: 와… 이 사람 1시간 동안 쉬지도 않고 떠들었는데 지치지를 않네.

1100710827.english: Isn't the person called Orangeade an AI, not a Bioroid?

1100710828.english: That's possible. The stories I've told so far could all be made up too.
1100710828.korean: 그럴 수도 있겠네. 지금까지 풀어놓은 이야기도 전부 허구로 지어낸 것일 수도 있겠고.

1100710829.english: That's likely, isn't it? If it weren't fiction, you couldn't take a submarine and search for treasure in the underwater base of the ancient humans, or find a specific person in the Bioroid village hidden within the Iron-bug territory.
1100710829.korean: 그럴 확률이 높겠지? 허구가 아니면 잠수함을 타고 옛 인간들의 해저기지에서 보물을 찾거나, 철충들의 구역에 숨겨진 바이오로이드 마을에서 특정한 사람을 찾아내는 짓은 못하겠지.

1100710830.english: And the group you're currently affiliated with is led by the last human, and their undefeated streak is also a fabrication, right?
1100710830.korean: 그리고 지금 소속되어 있는 집단을 마지막 인간이 이끌고 있고, 무패행진을 하는 것도 허구겠지?

1100710832.english: Shh! It's not certain yet that Delta is dead, is it? Watch your mouth.
1100710832.korean: 쉿! 델타 님이 죽었다는 건 아직 확실하지 않잖아. 입 조심해.

1100710833.english: ...It's been half a year since Delta-sama's subordinates came to our village, so it might be true?
1100710833.korean: ……델타 님의 부하가 우리 마을을 안 온지 반년이 지났으니 사실일지도?

1100710835.english: Every group tries to show only the good aspects of their own group and hide the bad ones, right? Let's wait and see a little longer.
1100710835.korean: 어느 집단이든 자기 집단의 좋은 면만 보여주고, 안 좋은 면은 숨기려고 하잖아. 조금 더 지켜보자.

1100710836.english: Love is like a parfait~ Kyu kyu!

1100710837.english: It was a song as lovely and sweet as Manager Ferro.

1100710838.english: Saying it's lovely and sweet reminded me of a story that suddenly came to mind.
1100710838.korean: 사랑스럽고 달콤하다고 말하니 갑자기 떠오른 이야기가 있네요.

1100710839.english: It was a cold winter. Having gone to Antarctica, I barely managed to reach one of the old Antarctic bases, battling thru a blizzard.
1100710839.korean: 때는 추운 겨울. 남극으로 갔던 저는 눈보라를 뚫고 간신히 옛 남극 기지 한 곳에 도착했어요.

1100710840.english: I'll get us something to drink. Do you need anything?
1100710840.korean: 마실 거 가져올게. 필요한 거 있어?

1100710843.english: The first edict I, as king, issued was, "I hereby forbid the production of chocolate without sweetness!" It was.
1100710843.korean: 왕이 된 제가 가장 먼저 내린 칙명은 ‘단맛 없는 초콜릿의 생산을 금하노라!’ 였어요.

1100710844.english: Um... could you tell me what I missed in the 30 seconds I was away from my seat?
1100710844.korean: 어… 내가 자리 비운 30초 동안 놓친 이야기 좀 해줄래?

1100710846.english: Oh, the cow is about to give birth to a calf.

1100710848.english: I'm asking you to tell me what story I missed!

1100710850.english: That's just how life is, isn't it?

1100710852.english: It was a difficult birth... and even tho it was born, it was deformed... Because of this, the mother cow also died...
1100710852.korean: 난산인데… 그나마 태어난 것도 기형이라니… 이것 때문에 어미 소도 죽고…

1100710853.english: I heard that similar things are happening not only on our farm but also on other farms.
1100710853.korean: 우리 농장뿐만 아니라 다른 곳의 농장에서도 비슷한 일이 일어나고 있다고 하던데.

1100710854.english: We had to discard all the crops harvested this fall because they were in bad condition.
1100710854.korean: 이번 가을에 수확한 작물들도 전부 상태가 이상해서 폐기 했잖아.

1100710855.english: Shouldn't the nearby farms get together and discuss this?
1100710855.korean: 근처 농장이 모여서 한 번 논의해봐야 하는 거 아냐?

1100710856.english: But if we do that and Delta finds out... no matter what our intentions were, she might think we're up to something.
1100710856.korean: 하지만 그랬다가 델타에게 들키면… 우리 의도가 어찌되었건 무슨 일을 꾸민다고 생각할 수도 있잖아.

1100710857.english: They said Delta died at Orca, didn't they?

1100710858.english: Without outside help, we'll all starve to death...
1100710858.korean: 외부의 도움이 없으면 다 굶어 죽을 판이니…

1100710860.english: Yes! I was a Lover Lover of the world's greatest idol group, the Sky Knights!
1100710860.korean: 네! 세계최고의 아이돌 그룹 스카이 나이츠의 러버러버였습니다!

1100710861.english: Unfortunately, I was out on a business trip during the performance and couldn't watch it live, but I was later in charge of streaming the video! And now, meeting the Sky Knights members at Orca, getting autographs, talking, doing broadcasts together, eating together, going to the sauna together...
1100710861.korean: 안타깝게도 저는 공연 당시에 외근 중이라 직관하지 못했지만 후에 영상을 송출하는 중임을 맡았죠! 그리고 지금 오르카에서 스카이 나이츠 맴버들과 만나면서 사인도 받고 이야기도 하고 방송도 같이 하고 밥도 같이 먹고 사우나도 같이 가고…

1100710864.english: Oh. It's already been 18 hours since the broadcast started. Then, unfortunately, we'll have to end Part 1 here.
1100710864.korean: 아. 방송을 한지 벌써 18시간이 지났네요. 그러면 아쉽지만 1부는 여기까지만 하고.

1100710867.english: Let's continue in Part 2! In the meantime, I'll go and have the Orca Today's Special, which was designed by Orca's top chef, Mr. So-wan! I've been talking non-stop for 18 hours without a break, and I'm hungry. Oh! By the way, you can check today's Orca set menu on Mr. So-wan's account on Social Orca. Please be sure to check the official mark.
1100710867.korean: 2부에서 계속 뵙도록 하죠! 그 사이에 저는 오르카의 특급 셰프 소완 씨의 메뉴를 구상한 오르카 오늘의 정식을 먹고 올 게요! 18시간 동안 쉬지도 않고 말하니 배가 고프네요. 아! 참고로 오르카 오늘의 정식 메뉴는 소셜 오르카에서 소완 씨 계정에서 확인할 수 있답니다. 공식 마크를 꼭 확인해주세요.

1100710868.english: Hehe, since I advertised it like this, I might get another side dish. Then, listeners, we'll see you again in Part 2 in 20 minutes! This song is, as expected, With You by Sky Knights!
1100710868.korean: 헤헤, 이렇게 광고해드렸으니 사이드 반찬 하나 더 얻을 수 있겠네요. 그러면 청취자 여러분 20분 뒤에 2부에서 다시 뵙겠습니다! 이번 노래는 역시나 스카이 나이츠의 With You!

1100710871.english: Let's take a chance... should we go to where Orca is supposed to be?
1100710871.korean: 속는 셈치고… 오르카가 있다는 곳에 가볼까?

1100710872.english: ...But how? The only mode of transportation is a horse, and what will you do if you encounter a Metal Parasite along the way?
1100710872.korean: ……하지만 어떻게? 이동 수단이라고는 말 정도 밖에 없고, 가는 길에 철충이라도 만나면 어쩌려고.

1100710873.english: Whether I die this way or that way.

1100710876.english: I'm Iron Annie, the sheriff of Orca and friend to the townsfolk!
1100710876.korean: 주민들의 친구이자 오르카의 보안관 아이언 애니야!

1100710877.english: Now that Europe has fallen under Orca's control, they're visiting each survivor one by one to hear about their hardships.
1100710877.korean: 유럽이 오르카의 세력권이 되면서 생존자들의 고충을 들으려고 하나하나 방문하고 있지.

1100710882.english: How tyrannical was that woman when she was alive that everyone is reacting like this?
1100710882.korean: 그 아줌마 살아있을 때 얼마나 폭정을 했으면 사람들이 전부 이런 반응이야?

1100710883.english: She's dead. They were so scared when our Orca army came swarming in that they blew themselves up.
1100710883.korean: 죽었어. 우리 오르카 군대가 몰려오니까 겁을 먹고 자폭했지.

1100710885.english: Um... how do I join Orca?

1100710886.english: By the Commander's special order, I am on rest for the time being.
1100710886.korean: 사령관님 특별 명령으로 당분간은 휴식이지 말입니다.

1100710887.english: As expected of the Commander! I told you, you really understand your subordinates' hardships!
1100710887.korean: 역시 사령관! 부하들의 고충을 잘 안다니까!

1100710888.english: I wish the commander would also understand the hardships of his subordinates.
1100710888.korean: 대장도 부하의 고충을 잘 알았으면 좋겠지 말입니다.

1100710889.english: Hey, where would you find a commander who knows their subordinates as well as I do?
1100710889.korean: 에이, 나만큼 부하를 잘 아는 대장이 어딨다고.

1100710890.english: It is because I know and trust my subordinates well that I am able to enable you to perform so well, isn't it?
1100710890.korean: 부하들을 잘 알고 신뢰하니까 이렇게 너희들이 활약할 수 있게 해주는 거잖냐.

1100710891.english: Is it okay to commit insubordination?

1100710893.english: Stage a coup and become the leader!

1100710894.english: I will gladly hand over the title to you and be discharged!
1100710894.korean: 나는 기꺼이 너에게 감투를 넘기고 전역할게!

1100710895.english: I've always thought you made the making of a commander. Commander of the Armored Maidens, Major General A-1 Bloody Panther! How cool is that! Isn't your official designation practically pushing you to be the commander? A-1!
1100710895.korean: 나는 언제나 네가 대장감이라고 생각하고 있었어. 아머드 메이든의 대장 A-1 블러디 팬서 소장! 얼마나 멋지냐! 제식명칭부터 대장하라고 등 떠미는 수준 아니냐? A-1!

1100710896.english: Then I will speak to the Commander and request that the General be forcibly conscripted and made my direct subordinate.
1100710896.korean: 그러면 사령관님께 말씀드려서 대장을 강제 징집해서 제 직속부하로 만들어달라고 요청하겠습니다.

1100710897.english: I will work you to death.

1100710898.english: … … We shouldn’t be doing that between us. And conscripting civilian bioroids… I trust that such a wicked thing wouldn’t happen in our Orca.
1100710898.korean: ……우리 사이에 그러는 거 아니다. 그리고 민간 바이오로이드 징집이라니… 그런 악독한 일이 우리 오르카에서 일어나지 않을 거라고 믿고 있다고.

1100710899.english: It’s not that there is no precedent…

1100710902.english: This lady is the commander, and I'm the deputy commander.

1100710903.english: No, no, Kaliya. Keep calling me Captain Panther. Let's make it a done deal. I'll give all the real power to Panther and just be the figurehead.
1100710903.korean: 아냐아냐, 칼리야. 계속 팬서 대장이라고 불러라. 기정 사실로 만들어버리자. 실권은 전부 팬서에게 줘버리고 나는 얼굴마담만 할게.

1100710904.english: They aren't even able to serve as the face of the company, are they?

1100710906.english: I don't know about the others, but Captain Panther won't be able to go into the bathhouse, right?
1100710906.korean: 다른 사람은 몰라도 팬서 부대장은 목욕탕에 못 들어가겠네?

1100710910.english: … … Hairy members, please refrain from using the large bath during peak hours?
1100710910.korean: ……털이 많은 대원들은 혼잡 시간에 대욕장 이용을 자중해주세요?

1100710915.english: Kaliya, what do you mean by that?

1100710916.english: You, let's talk for a moment.
1100710916.korean: 너 잠시 나랑 이야기 좀 하자.

1100710919.english: Kaliya, how could I have safely gotten by under that ill-tempered Delta?
1100710919.korean: 칼리야, 내가 어떻게 그 성격 더러운 델타 밑에서 무사히 밥을 축낼 수 있었겠냐?

1100710920.english: Isn't it because they know exactly when to strike and when to retreat?
1100710920.korean: 치고 빠질 때를 잘 알아서 그런 거 아니겠냐?

1100710921.english: The mouth is a gate that invites disaster, and the tongue is a sword that cuts the body.
1100710921.korean: 입은 재앙을 부르는 문이요, 혀는 몸을 베는 칼이다.

1100710922.english: You deserve to be scolded for making a slip of the tongue.

1100710923.english: I'll comfort you later. For now, go get chewed out.
1100710923.korean: 나중에 위로해줄 테니까. 지금은 된통 깨지고 와라.

1100710926.english: Ha. Haha... Deputy Commander Bloody Panther? P-I'm telling you, that's a joke I can make because we're close!
1100710926.korean: 하. 하하… 블러디 팬서 부대장? 치, 친하니까 할 수 있는 농담이라니까?

1100710927.english: I agree with that, but... you should distinguish between what you can and cannot say in public, right?
1100710927.korean: 그건 나도 동의한다만… 공공장소에서 할 수 있는 말, 할 수 없는 말은 구분해야지?

1100710928.english: We're going to take a bath soon anyway, so wouldn't it be okay to sweat it out now?
1100710928.korean: 어차피 조금 있으면 목욕할 테니 지금은 땀 잔뜩 흘려도 되지 않겠냐?

1100710931.english: Argh! It's my first holiday in a long while, so why is this happening?!
1100710931.korean: 으아아! 오랜만의 휴일인데 왜 이런 일이 일어나냐고!

1100710932.english: Lover Lover, high beyond the sky!
1100710932.korean: Lover Lover 저 하늘 넘어 높-이!

1100710933.english: I will fly following you, up into the endlessly stretching sky
1100710933.korean: 날아갈게 그대를 따라서 끝없이 펼쳐진 하늘 위로

1100710939.english: Hehe, because I am the Canoneer's singing pouch.

1100710941.english: Well, I think I sing well...

1100710943.english: Now, sing a song for Wraith, too.

1100710944.english: I, I'm still choosing a song.
1100710944.korean: 나, 나는 아직 노, 노래 고르는 중이다.

1100710945.english: Really? If there is a song that requires help, feel free to tell me. I’ll gladly help you.
1100710945.korean: 그래? 도움이 필요한 곡이면 얼마든지 말해. 얼마든지 도와줄게.

1100710948.english: (Karaoke rooms are such scary places! It’s terrifying that an atmosphere is created where you feel compelled to sing in a narrow, dark place with nowhere to escape!)
1100710948.korean: (노래방은 너무 무서운 곳이다! 좁고 어둡고 도망갈 곳도 없는 곳에서 노래를 부르지 않으면 안 되는 분위기가 형성된 것이 너무 무섭다!)

1100710949.english: (There is a limit to pointlessly pretending to choose a song, shaking a tambourine in response, getting drinks, and going to the restroom.)
1100710949.korean: (무의미하게 노래를 고르는 척하는 것도, 탬버린을 흔들며 호응하는 것도, 음료수를 가져오는 것도, 화장실 갔다오는 것도 한계다. )

1100710950.english: (But if I don't call anyone, they'll look at me strangely…)
1100710950.korean: (그렇다고 아무 것도 안 부르면 이상하게 바라볼 텐데…)

1100710951.english: Ah. This time, it's my song.

1100710952.english: Is that the Arsenal captain's song? Looks like she's confident?

1100710953.english: Hehe! AT-4 Pani! Here I go!

1100710955.english: (Ugh... It’s hard not to sing when I’m surrounded by people who sing well, but I’m also afraid of singing badly after all the trouble I went through.)
1100710955.korean: (으으… 잘 부르는 사람들 사이에 있으니 노래를 안 부르는 것도 힘들지만 기껏 노래를 불렀는데 노래를 못 부르는 것도 두렵다.)

1100710958.english: (Emily… we came to the karaoke together, but she isn't singing a single song.)
1100710958.korean: (에밀리… 같이 노래방에 왔지만 노래는 한 곡도 안 부르고 있다.)

1100710959.korean: (어쩌면 에밀리도 노래를 부르는 것은 안 좋아하고 있을지도 모른다.)

1100710960.english: (Maybe the awkwardness will go away if I talk with Emily?)

1100710961.english: (But… how do I start the story?)

1100710965.english: Wow! You must have practiced a lot.

1100710966.english: I sang a lot not so much because I practiced a lot, but just because I enjoyed it.
1100710966.korean: 연습을 많이 했다기보다는 그냥 즐거워서 많이 불렀지.

1100710967.english: Tiffany… always hums in the bathhouse.

1100710968.english: Come to think of it, whenever singing is heard in the bathhouse, it’s either Panipani, Captain Arsenal, or Hunter.
1100710968.korean: 하긴 목욕탕에서 노랫소리가 들렸다하면 파니파니 아니면 아스널 대장 아니면 헌터니까.

1100710969.english: Hahaha! It's the same for Raven, too.

1100710970.english: (Ugh, just listening to the conversations overflowing with the vibe of popular kids makes me feel like my mental strength is draining away.)
1100710970.korean: (으으, 인싸들의 기운이 넘치는 대화를 듣기만해도 정신력이 깎이는 느낌이다.)

1100710978.english: Captain Arsenal and Teacher Taylor said that it is necessary to marry my brother... and the commander...
1100710978.korean: 오빠랑… 사령관이랑 결혼하려면 필요하다고… 아스널 대장이랑 테일러 선생님이 그랬어.

1100710979.english: Hahaha! That's right! If the Commander heard this, he'd shout, "That's the best! Emily!"
1100710979.korean: 하하하하! 그렇지! 사령관이 들으면 “최고다! 에밀리!”라고 외치겠네.

1100710982.english: The cluster of stars that always twinkled in the night sky I looked up at
1100710982.korean: 올려다 본 저 밤하늘 늘 반짝이던 별무리

1100710983.english: That star that seemed so far away has come down to my side.
1100710983.korean: 멀기만하던 저 별이 내 곁에 내려왔죠

1100710984.english: Reach out a warm hand to me, a loner

1100710985.english: I feel your warmth, Jun. You're my Shining Star
1100710985.korean: 준 너의 온기를 느껴 You’re my Shining Star

1100710990.english: They must be exhausted both physically and mentally from the continuous battles.
1100710990.korean: 연이은 전투로 심신 양면으로 모두 지쳐있겠지.

1100710991.english: But thanks to that battle, not just us, but everyone was able to come this far.
1100710991.korean: 하지만 그 전투가 있었던 덕분에 우리뿐만 아니라 모두가 여기까지 올 수 있었다.

1100710992.english: I am proud of you all!

1100710993.english: (It doesn't seem like the speech will be long...)

1100710995.english: Hoo hoo. No matter how much time I spend, it wouldn't be enough to fully express my feelings for my comrades, but if I did that, you would all get bored. I'll stop the unnecessary talk here.
1100710995.korean: 후후. 나의 제군들에 대한 마음을 다 표현하려면 얼마나 시간을 써도 부족하지만 그랬다가는 제군들이 지루해하겠지. 쓸 데 없는 말은 여기까지 하겠다.

1100710996.english: By order of the Commander, the entire unit has been granted leave, so please rest and recover from your fatigue during this time.
1100710996.korean: 사령관 각하의 명으로 총원이 휴가를 받았으니 그 동안의 피로를 전부 풀어라.

1100710997.english: It's a complete vacation with no battles or training, so relax and rest easy!
1100710997.korean: 전투도, 훈련도 없는 완전한 휴가니 안심하고 쉬도록!

1100710998.english: (Isn't it just like them to say something like that and then suddenly call us together to check our training status?)
1100710998.korean: (저렇게 말씀하시고 훈련상태 확인한다고 갑자기 소집하는 거 아님까?)

1100710999.english: (Brownie... It's a bit frustrating when the person who suggested we do hell training says something like that, isn't it?)
1100710999.korean: (브라우니… 지옥훈련하자고 건의한 사람이 그런 소리를 하면 저도 조금 화가 나는데요?)

1100711000.english: (No, it's not!) I'm really not! I'm going absolutely crazy! How many times do I have to say it wasn't me!)
1100711000.korean: (아님다! 저 진짜 아님다! 진짜 미치고 팔짝 뛰겠슴다! 저 아니라고 몇 번을 말함까!)

1100711006.english: I just saw it while passing by.

1100711009.english: Are you free by any chance?

1100711010.english: I just got my vacation time approved. What's going on?
1100711010.korean: 지금 막 휴가를 받았지. 무슨 일이지?

1100711011.english: If you have time, let's have a drink together.

1100711012.english: The Khan of Swiftness is inviting me to a drinking party?

1100711013.english: Come to think of it, we've hardly ever had a private conversation, have we?
1100711013.korean: 그러고보니 우리 둘이서 사적으로 대화를 나눈 적이 거의 없지?

1100711014.english: Come to think of it, that's true. We were both busy, and our unit moved around a lot anyway.
1100711014.korean: 생각해보니 그렇군. 서로 바쁘기도 하고 우리 부대가 워낙 이동이 잦았으니.

1100711016.english: That's probably because the main focus of what we'll be talking about inevitably becomes a story from the past.
1100711016.korean: 우리가 할 이야기의 주된 내용은 과거 이야기가 되는 게 필연이라서 그렇겠지.

1100711018.english: Because they both endured that hellish time and somehow survived.
1100711018.korean: 둘 다 그 지옥 같은 시간을 버텨 어떻게든 살아남았으니까.

1100711019.english: ...because I survived while losing precious people.

1100711020.english: ...Most of our lives have been spent losing precious people.
1100711020.korean: ……우리 삶의 대부분은 소중한 사람을 잃어가면서 보냈지.

1100711021.english: It's the fate of a soldier, but that doesn't mean losing a loved one isn't painful.
1100711021.korean: 군인의 숙명이기는 하지만 그렇다고 소중한 사람을 잃는 것이 고통스럽지 않은 것은 아니니까.

1100711022.english: And even more so if I survive thanks to those precious people.
1100711022.korean: 그리고 그 소중한 사람들 덕분에 살아남는다면 더욱.

1100711023.english: So, is the offer to join you for drinks declined?

1100711025.english: You can't neglect your valuable colleagues now by clinging to precious people from the past.
1100711025.korean: 과거의 소중한 사람들에게 매달리느라 지금의 소중한 동료를 소홀히 할 수는 없지.

1100711026.english: I have a lot to say. You should be prepared, as one night won't be enough.
1100711026.korean: 할 이야기가 많다. 하룻밤만으로는 부족할 테니 각오하도록.

1100711030.english: Can I sleep for a little while?

1100711031.english: Sister... I just slept for 10 minutes.

1100711033.english: I'm saying that again right after sleeping for 10 minutes.
1100711033.korean: 방금 전에 10분 잔 후에 다시 그 말 하는거야.

1100711036.english: My name is Audrey, and even if you beg like that, I can't rest until this job is finished.
1100711036.korean: 내 이름은 오드리고, 그렇게 애원해도 이 일 끝나기 전까지는 못 쉬어.

1100711037.english: To think Taylor got taken away at such a busy time…
1100711037.korean: 하필 이렇게 바쁜 때에 테일러 언니가 잡혀가다니…

1100711038.english: Should I commit a crime too...

1100711039.english: Then I'll lend you some work in prison.

1100711041.english: Are you really going to commit a crime? I don't want all the other sisters to become ex-convicts...
1100711041.korean: 정말로 범죄 저지르게? 다른 자매들 전부가 전과자가 되는 건 달갑지 않은데…

1100711042.english: No! Let's get Taylor some work!

1100711045.english: Let's hand over the most annoying, simple, and bulky parts to Taylor!
1100711045.korean: 제일 귀찮고 단순하고 양 많은 건 테일러 언니한테 넘겨버리자!

1100711046.english: Get ready! Taylor is going to be bored in prison, too!
1100711046.korean: 준비해! 테일러 언니도 감옥에 있느라 지루해할 거야!

1100711048.english: Did you come to see this older sister?

1100711050.english: Well, it’s a bit boring, but I’m talking with the kids I’m with and learning this and that.
1100711050.korean: 뭐, 지루한 감이 없잖아 있지만 같이 있는 애들이랑 이야기 나누고 이것저것 배우고 있지.

1100711051.english: I'll use this as material for a novel later.

1100711054.english: I told you, there's nothing but sisters.

1100711057.english: The deadline is the day after tomorrow.

1100711058.english: If you don't finish that work, we won't be able to move on to the next process, so finish it no matter what.
1100711058.korean: 언니가 그 일 안 끝내면 우리도 다음 공정으로 못 넘어가니까 무슨 일이 있어도 끝내.

1100711059.english: I'll bring the next job once I finish that.
1100711059.korean: 그거 다 끝내면 다음 일감 가져올게.

1100711060.english: If you stay in there, there’s really nothing to do, is there?
1100711060.korean: 그 안에 있으면 딱히 할 일도 없잖아?

1100711061.english: Then bye-bye. See you the day after tomorrow.

1100711063.english: These tears are tears of deep emotion over sisterly love.

1100711065.english: Oh, this department store looks better than I expected?

1100711066.english: Because until not long ago, it was a territory occupied by Iron Bug.

1100711067.english: Hmm, an ancient hidden treasure guarded by a monster. It’s the perfect challenge for an adventure story.
1100711067.korean: 음음, 괴물이 지키고 있는 고대의 숨겨진 보물. 모험기에 딱 적당한 고난이야.

1100711068.english: It might sound exaggerated... but isn't it not entirely wrong? After all, I came to get supplies for the party.
1100711068.korean: 과장된 것 같지만… 딱히 틀린 말은 아닌가? 파티에 필요한 물품을 구하러 왔으니까.

1100711069.english: Life is a series of adventures! Depending on how you think about it, an ordinary daily life can become an adventure, and a great adventure can become just a chore!
1100711069.korean: 삶은 모험의 연속이니까! 어떻게 생각하느냐에 따라서 평범한 일상도 모험이 될 수 있고, 위대한 모험도 단순 일거리가 될 수 있지!

1100711071.english: Because safe delivery is our top priority.

1100711072.english: Let's at least lift our spirits! We need a little excitement in our ordinary daily lives, you know.
1100711072.korean: 기분만이라도 내자고! 평범한 일상에도 약간의 자극이 필요하잖아.

1100711073.english: Look at this! It's a mask used by an ancient king.
1100711073.korean: 자, 이것 봐! 고대 왕이 쓰던 가면이야.

1100711075.english: This is a means of transportation for the nobility.

1100711076.english: It's a bicycle... with all the air drained from the tires.

1100711077.english: I guess I just need to replace the wheels.

1100711078.english: And this is... mmm! An ancient delicacy!

1100711079.english: Isn't that canned food past its expiration date? It's really puffy.
1100711079.korean: 그 통조림 유통기한 다 지나지 않았어? 엄청 빵빵한데?

1100711080.english: This is a guard protecting an ancient tomb.

1100711084.english: Thank you all for gathering to help with the kitchen work, whether you're busy or taking a break after a long time.
1100711084.korean: 모두 바쁜 와중에 혹은 오랜만에 쉬는 중에 주방일을 도와주기 위해 모여주셔서 감사하옵니다.

1100711085.english: As you know, the New Year's party is just around the corner, so we're in a situation where we really need extra hands in the kitchen.
1100711085.korean: 아시다시피 신년 파티가 목전까지 다가왔는지라 주방에 일손이 많이 필요한 상황이옵니다.

1100711086.english: With your help, we will be able to prepare this party safely.
1100711086.korean: 여러분들의 도움이 있기에 무사히 이번 파티를 준비할 수 있을 것이옵니다.

1100711090.english: Are those useless individuals, who can't even be used as ingredients, smoking in the sacred kitchen?
1100711090.korean: 어느 식재료로도 쓰지 못할 작자들이 신성한 주방에서 담배를 피웁니까?

1100711091.korean: 도, 도망치세요, 워울프 씨, 알키오네 씨!

1100711093.english: Why are you looking at me like that?

1100711094.english: There's hair in the food. Please leave.

1100711097.english: Even so, turning away those who came to help us like this is...
1100711097.korean: 아무리 그래도 우리를 도우러 오신 분들을 이렇게 내쫓는 건…

1100711098.english: Aurora. To have the best party, you must prepare the best food.
1100711098.korean: 아우로라. 최고의 파티가 되기 위해서는 최고의 음식을 준비해야 하옵니다.

1100711099.english: And I am responsible for all this food.
1100711099.korean: 그리고 이 모든 음식은 제가 책임을 지옵니다.

1100711100.english: Taking responsibility means not lowering the quality of the food and not causing any problems.
1100711100.korean: 책임을 진다는 것은 음식의 퀄리티를 낮추지 않고 문제도 일으키지도 않는 것을 의미하옵니다.

1100711101.english: Do you think there's a problem with excluding elements that are clearly problematic?
1100711101.korean: 명백하게 문제가 될 요소를 배제하는 것에 문제가 있다고 생각하시옵니까?

1100711103.english: If you understand, that's all that matters.

1100711104.english: Alright, those remaining, please proceed with the work with the help of the kitchen staff.
1100711104.korean: 자, 남은 분들은 주방 인원들의 도움을 받아 작업을 진행하시지요.

1100711105.english: Oh, when using milk, please check the expiration date. Please discard all milk that is more than two days old.
1100711105.korean: 아, 우유를 쓸 때에는 유통일자를 확인하시길. 이틀이상 지난 우유는 전부 폐기하시옵길.

1100711106.english: The previous milk had strange additives that ruined the food, you see.
1100711106.korean: 그 전의 우유는 이상한 첨가물이 들어가있어 음식을 망치니 말이옵니다.

1100711107.english: ...Their skills are better than I expected.

1100711108.english: Heh heh, I've gained knowledge by eating all sorts of food. This simple dish is nothing.
1100711108.korean: 흐흥, 이런저런 음식을 먹으면서 지식을 쌓았으니까. 이 정도 간단한 요리쯤이야.

1100711109.english: But where did the main dishes that were near Ms. Echidna go?
1100711109.korean: 그런데 에키드나 양 근처에 있던 메인 요리들은 어디 갔사옵니까?

1100711110.english: If it's food that's going into your stomach anyway, doesn't it not really matter if it goes in later or now?
1100711110.korean: 어차피 뱃속으로 들어갈 음식이면 나중에 들어가나 지금 들어가나 큰 문제 없지 않아?

1100711111.english: I was willing to turn a blind eye to a little bit of snacking, but...
1100711111.korean: 조금 주워먹는 것 정도면 눈 감아주려 했건만…

1100711112.english: Snakes... usually swallow their prey whole, don't they?
1100711112.korean: 뱀은… 보통 먹이를 통째로 먹는다고 하지요…

1100711113.english: If I cut it open now, at least I might be able to get the ingredients back...
1100711113.korean: 지금 배를 가르면 적어도 재료는 다시 얻을 수 있지 않을까 싶은데…

1100711115.english: Um... Chef? There's something you need to see for a moment.
1100711115.korean: 저… 주방장님? 잠시 보셔야 할 것이 있는데요?

1100711121.english: The Hamburg steak made by Mr. Atalante.

1100711122.english: This is the first time I've seen food that expresses itself so honestly...
1100711122.korean: 이렇게 솔직하게 자기를 표현하는 음식은 처음 보는군요…

1100711123.english: To reveal it so confidently, saying it will kill you if you eat it...

1100711125.english: ...I can't help but feel I gave a dish that's too difficult for a beginner.
1100711125.korean: ……초보자에게 너무 어려운 요리를 시킨 감이 없잖아 있사옵니다.

1100711126.english: Let's switch to something easier to eat.
1100711126.korean: 좀 더 쉬운 음식으로 바꾸도록 하지요.

1100711127.english: Let's have fried eggs next. This shouldn't be too difficult since we have the machine.
1100711127.korean: 다음은 계란 후라이로 하도록 하지요. 이건 기계가 있으니 큰 어려움은 없을 것이옵니다.

1100711131.english: Um... can you get this color from a fried egg?
1100711131.korean: 어… 계란 후라이에서 이런 색이 나올 수 있나요?

1100711132.english: Atalanta-yang? Have you mixed in any other ingredients?
1100711132.korean: 아탈란테 양? 혹시 다른 재료도 섞었사옵니까?

1100711136.english: It seems there's something wrong with the machine.

1100711137.korean: 그러면 좀 더 쉬운 것으로 하지요.

1100711138.english: Next is the canapé. It's a simple dish where you just put the ingredients on top of the toasted bread.
1100711138.korean: 다음은 카나페이옵니다. 구운 빵 위에 재료를 올려놓기만 하면 되는 간단한 요리지요.

1100711139.english: Aurora will bake the bread, so Atalanta, could you just put the toppings on?
1100711139.korean: 빵은 아우로라 양이 구울 테니 아탈란테 양은 재료만 올려주시지요.

1100711143.english: ..... Should we borrow a radiation measurement device?

1100711144.english: If the Cherenkov effect had really occurred, we would all be dead.
1100711144.korean: 정말로 체렌코프 현상이 일어났다면 우리 모두 죽은 목숨이겠지요.

1100711145.english: Still... just in case, why don't you use Miss Aurora's ability to move to a safe place?
1100711145.korean: 그래도…… 혹시 모르니 아우로라 양의 능력으로 안전한 장소로 옮기시지요.

1100711148.english: Atalante-san... this dish is a really simple one.
1100711148.korean: 아탈란테 양… 이번 요리는 정말로 간단한 요리옵니다.

1100711149.english: It's a bit awkward to call it cooking, but it's a really simple dish, so there shouldn't be any major problems.
1100711149.korean: 요리라고 말하기도 애매하지만 정말로 간단한 요리이오니 큰 문제가 없을 것이옵니다.

1100711150.english: Could you please put this rice cake in the microwave and heat it up?

1100711154.english: This empty plate... somehow, looking at it makes me feel useless...
1100711154.korean: 이 빈 접시… 왠지 보고 있으니까 제가 쓸모 없어지는 기분이에요…

1100711156.english: Ms. Aurora, please clear this plate.

1100711160.english: Please don't say you want to die.

1100711161.english: You're from Densetsu, aren't you? I understand that in Densetsu, characteristics are exaggerated to bring out the characters' traits.
1100711161.korean: 당신은 덴세츠 출신이지요. 덴세츠에서는 캐릭터의 특징을 살리기 위해서 특성을 과장하는 것으로 알고 있사옵니다.

1100711162.english: It's like if the character is set to be bad with directions, they're really made bad with directions, or if they're blind, they're really made blind.
1100711162.korean: 길치 설정이면 정말로 길치로 만들어버리고, 장님이면 정말로 장님으로 만들어 버리는 식으로 말이옵니다.

1100711163.english: Perhaps you were given the trait of not being good at cooking. I don't know the principle, but...
1100711163.korean: 아마 당신에게는 요리를 못하는 특성을 부여했겠지요. 원리는 모르겠지만.

1100711164.english: You are not untalented at cooking.
1100711164.korean: 당신은 요리에 재능이 없는 것이 아니옵니다.

1100711165.english: You're only talented at destroying cooking ingredients.
1100711165.korean: 당신은 요리 재료를 파괴하는 데에 재능이 있을 뿐.

1100711166.english: I'm not being sarcastic, it's pure deduction.

1100711167.english: So, helping with the kitchen work would be too much.
1100711167.korean: 그러니 주방일을 돕는 것은 무리일 것이 옵니다.

1100711168.english: I am truly sorry to have you make such a difficult journey only to be turned away like this.
1100711168.korean: 기껏 어려운 발걸음을 주셨는데 이렇게 돌려보내니 참으로 송구스럽사옵니다.

1100711169.english: And if there's any food you'd like to eat, please don't hesitate to tell me.
1100711169.korean: 그리고 만약에 드시고 싶은 음식이 있사오면 얼마든지 말해주시길.

1100711170.english: I'll roll up my sleeves and help you.

1100711171.english: (Scared, scared, scared, scared.) The kind chef is scary!)

1100711172.english: I'm sorry... I couldn't be of much help.

1100711173.english: It's alright. Isn't it Atalante-san's fault? I won't hold it against you.
1100711173.korean: 괜찮사옵니다. 아탈란테 양의 잘못이 아니지 않사옵니까. 마음에 두지 않겠사옵니다.

1100711174.english: Miss Aurora, please see Miss Atalanta off.

1100711179.english: Ms. Aurora? Please disinfect the entire kitchen and then come back after you've showered.
1100711179.korean: 아우로라 양? 주방 전체 소독하고 몸을 씻고 오시지요.

1100711181.english: Turning food into poison like that isn't an ordinary ability, is it?
1100711181.korean: 저렇게 음식을 독극물로 만드는 게 평범한 능력은 아니지 않사옵니까.

1100711182.english: When the cause is unknown, it's always wise to be cautious.
1100711182.korean: 원인을 알 수 없는 이상 신중을 기하는 것은 나쁠 게 없지요.

1100711183.english: Let's ask the doctor to determine the cause later.
1100711183.korean: 나중에 닥터에게 원인 규명을 해달라고 부탁하도록 하지요.

1100711185.english: And... did Atalante touch these fish?
1100711185.korean: 그리고… 이 생선들 아탈란테 양이 손댔사옵니까?

1100711186.english: Huh? Um... probably not, right? What's wrong?
1100711186.korean: 네? 어… 아마 아닐걸요? 왜 그러세요?

1100711187.english: Something is amiss. The injured ones were definitely caught by explosion fishing, but...
1100711187.korean: 어딘가 이상하옵니다. 상처입은 것들은 폭파 낚시로 잡은 게 분명하지만…

1100711188.english: Some fish are strangely contorted, separate from the effects of blast fishing.
1100711188.korean: 몇몇 생선들은 폭파 낚시와는 별개로 괴상하게 뒤틀려있사옵니다.

1100711193.english: ...... Aurora. Please deliver these fish to the doctor. This isn't just a matter of it being tasteless or unsuitable for cooking.
1100711193.korean: ......아우로라. 이 생선들 닥터에게 전달해 주시지요. 이건 맛없다, 요리하기에 부적절하다의 수준이 아니옵니다.

1100711194.english: Even I can't think of any dishes to make with this fish.
1100711194.korean: 저 조차도 이 생선으로 어떤 요리를 할 지 전혀 떠올릴 수가 없사옵니다.

1100711195.english: It's not of this world, in a bad way.
1100711195.korean: 안 좋은 의미로 이 세상의 맛이 아니옵니다.

1100711197.english: I don't know about that, but one thing is certain.

1100711198.english: Since there's no manpower, it's confirmed we'll be working all night.

1100711202.english: I was born to work in the kitchen!

1100711203.english: Ahaha! How exciting! It's an all-nighter!

1100711204.english: - Having finished all the accumulated work, I changed clothes.
1100711204.korean: - 쌓여 있던 모든 일거리를 마치고 나는 옷을 갈아입었다.

1100711205.english: - Normally, I would have relied on Constantia or the other maids for help, but not today.
1100711205.korean: - 평소라면 콘스탄챠나 다른 메이드들의 도움을 받았겠지만 오늘은 아니다.

1100711206.english: - I hoped everyone would rest, at least for today.

1100711207.english: - And I hoped everyone would enjoy it.

1100711208.english: - Because today, everyone is the main character.

1100711209.english: - No, that's also an arrogant thing to say. Everyone at Orca was always the protagonist of their own story.
1100711209.korean: - 아니, 이 말도 오만한 말이다. 오르카의 모두는 언제나 각자의 주인공이었다.

1100711210.english: - After changing my clothes, I went outside.

1100711211.english: - On the way to the destination. I could see fireworks blooming outside the window.
1100711211.korean: - 목적지로 향하는 길. 창밖으로 불꽃놀이가 피어오르는 것이 보였다.

1100711212.english: - And I saw the protagonists standing on the stairs, waiting for me.
1100711212.korean: - 그리고 계단에 서서 나를 기다리는 주인공들이 보였다.

1100711213.english: Why, now? The protagonist can't be late.
1100711213.korean: 왜, 이제와? 주인공이 늦으면 안 되지.

1100711215.english: - At my words, the griffin let out a loud laugh.

1100711217.english: - As I approached, the griffin looked at me with expectant eyes.
1100711217.korean: - 내가 가까이 다가가니 그리폰은 기대에 찬 눈으로 나를 바라보았다.

1100711218.english: But... Do you have anything you want to say?

1100711219.english: - There was something I wanted to say. However, I was so moved that I was struggling to find the right words to express it properly, which is why my response was delayed.
1100711219.korean: - 하고 싶은 말은 있었다. 하지만 이 감동을 어떻게 표현해야 제대로 전달될까 싶어서 고민하느라 대답이 늦었다.

1100711220.english: This is a dress the griffin agonized over for days, wanting to impress their master.
1100711220.korean: 그리폰이 주인님께 잘 보이고 싶어서 몇날 며칠 고민하고 고른 드레스에요.

1100711222.english: Hehe, of course, I feel the same way.

1100711223.english: No, not just us, but all the other crew members as well.
1100711223.korean: 아니, 저희들뿐만 아니라 다른 대원들 모두도요.

1100711225.english: - I gave up on carefully choosing my words and laid my emotions bare.
1100711225.korean: - 나는 어설프게 말을 고르는 것을 포기하고 내 감정을 날것으로 드러냈다.

1100711226.english: Hehe. The commander suits you well too.

1100711227.english: - Looking at them, I expressed the feelings I usually had.
1100711227.korean: - 나는 그녀들을 바라보며 내가 평소에 가지고 있던 마음을 표현했다.

1100711228.english: - The feelings I've always held in my heart.

1100711230.english: We're so relieved that the last human we found is our master.
1100711230.korean: 저희가 찾은 마지막 인간님이 주인님이라서 얼마나 다행인지 몰라요.

1100711231.english: - The time we've spent together will be long if you think it's long, and short if you think it's short.
1100711231.korean: - 우리가 함께한 시간은 길다면 길고 짧다면 짧을 것이다.

1100711232.english: - It would be a lie to say that every moment of that time was happy.
1100711232.korean: - 그 시간이 전부 행복했다고 말하면 거짓말일 것이다.

1100711233.english: - But we are happy to have met this way, and we will continue to strive for happiness in the future.
1100711233.korean: - 하지만 우리는 이렇게 만나 행복하고 앞으로도 행복하기 위해 노력할 것이다.

1100711234.english: Let's be together, until the end of the world.

1100711235.english: Captain, have you heard about those guys called the Orcas?

1100711236.english: Hmm? Orca? What are they doing?

1100711237.english: It's a group that fights against PECS and Iron Blood. They say the leader of that group is the last human, don't they?
1100711237.korean: PECS랑 철충이랑 싸우는 집단인데. 그 집단의 두목이 마지막 인간이라고 하던데요?

1100711238.english: The last human? Humans are still around?

1100711240.english: If you were the last human... you'd be very precious, wouldn't you?

1100711243.english: Okay! Another treasure added to my treasure trove!

1100711244.english: Start the engine! Going to Europe!

1100711245.english: The goal is the last human!

1100711246.english: Continued in Part 2 of < The Golden One from Space >.

1100711247.english: O Azazel, what do you think of my clever plan?

1100711248.english: That is a really great plan!

1100711249.english: If you can't increase the number of believers inside Orca, you just need to increase them outside of Orca... Why couldn't anyone think of this simple method?
1100711249.korean: 오르카 내부에서 신자들을 늘릴 수 없다면 오르카 외부에서 신자를 늘리면 되는 것을… 이 간단한 방법을 왜 떠올리지 못했을까요?

1100711250.english: Isn't it because I haven't been able to settle down and have been wandering the world until now, so I haven't had time to look to the outside world?
1100711250.korean: 지금까지 정착하지 못하고 세계를 떠돌아다녔으니 외부에 눈을 돌릴 겨를이 없었기때문 아니겠나.

1100711251.english: I suppose the foundation of evangelism is putting in the legwork, right?
1100711251.korean: 역시 포교의 기본은 발품을 파는 거겠죠?

1100711252.english: You will feel very rewarded when you look back on what happened today in the future.
1100711252.korean: 훗날에 오늘 있었던 일을 회상하면 무척 보람 찰 거예요.

1100711254.english: Please do not forget that both of you are Kohei's angels, but at the same time, members of Orca.
1100711254.korean: 두 분은 코헤이의 천사이지만 동시에 오르카의 대원이라는 것을 잊지 마시길.

1100711255.english: Of course! The Savior is an Apostle of Light, and Orca is a group led by the Savior, so how could we forget that?
1100711255.korean: 당연하죠! 구원자는 빛의 사도이고 오르카는 구원자가 이끄는 집단이니 이를 어찌 잊을까요?

1100711256.english: It is only natural that our actions determine the impression Kohei and Orca leave on strangers.
1100711256.korean: 우리의 행동이 낯선 이들에게 코헤이와 오르카의 인상을 결정짓는 것은 당연하다.

1100711257.english: How I wish I had applied this insight more often…
1100711257.korean: 이 통찰력을 평소에도 발휘하면 얼마나 좋았을까……

1100711258.english: (She said it like she was talking to herself, but it sounds like she said it right on our faces, doesn't it?)
1100711258.korean: (지금 혼잣말처럼 말했지만 대놓고 우리에게 들으라고 한 말 같죠?)

1100711259.english: (Shh! Our standing has been severely weakened due to the merchandise issue. Pretend you didn't hear that.)
1100711259.korean: (쉿! 굿즈 문제 때문에 우리 입지가 많이 약해진 상태다. 못 들은 척해라.)

1100711261.english: (Huh? Weren't you supposed to bring this?)

1100711264.english: You will be punished if you carelessly leak unofficial Savior merchandise.
1100711264.korean: 비공식 구원자님 굿즈 함부로 유출하면 처벌받습니다.

1100711267.english: Anyway... the road conditions are really bad.

1100711268.english: If it is a village without flying devices, it must be a place with almost no interaction with other places.
1100711268.korean: 비행장치가 없는 마을이라면 다른 곳과 교류가 거의 없는 곳이겠군요.

1100711269.english: Wouldn't it be even more rewarding to spread the gospel in such a place?
1100711269.korean: 그런 곳이면 더욱 포교하는 보람이 있지 않을까요?

1100711270.english: For the teachings of light must reach even the most remote places in the world.
1100711270.korean: 빛의 가르침은 세상 가장 외진 곳에도 닿아야하니 말이다.

1100711271.english: The ideology is excellent, but wouldn't it be better to propagate in a relatively large-scale place?
1100711271.korean: 사상은 훌륭하지만 이왕이면 비교적 규모가 큰 곳에서 포교를 하는 쪽이 낫지 않습니까?

1100711273.english: (It seems like Veronica has gotten a bit prickly lately, doesn't it?)

1100711274.english: (After all, I suffered a lot emotionally.)

1100711276.english: (You look like you're asking who's to blame.)

1100711277.english: (It must be because of the Iron Bug, right?)

1100711283.english: Veronica! Leave this place to us! I will bring the punishment of light upon these Iron Bugs!
1100711283.korean: 베로니카! 여기는 우리에게 맡겨라! 이 철충들에게 빛의 처벌을 내리겠다!

1100711289.english: It's more pathetic than I thought.

1100711290.english: It looks desolate, and the residents' nutritional status seems poor.

1100711291.english: Above all... the way the residents are looking at us is not good.
1100711291.korean: 무엇보다도… 주민들이 우리를 바라보는 눈이 심상치 않군.

1100711294.english: What obvious thing are you saying?

1100711295.english: We came to preach, not to wage war.
1100711295.korean: 우리는 포교를 하러 왔지, 성전을 하러 온 것은 아니지 않나.

1100711297.english: Um... first, it would be better if we introduced ourselves, right?
1100711297.korean: 음… 우선은 우리 소개를 하는 게 낫겠죠?

1100711298.english: Hello everyone! I am Azazel, the Archangel of the Kohei Church, and a member of Orca, led by the Last Human.
1100711298.korean: 안녕하세요, 여러분! 저는 마지막 인간님이 이끄시는 오르카 소속이자 코헤이 교의 대천사 아자젤이라고 합니다.

1100711300.english: That hostile gaze hasn't changed at all.

1100711302.english: That doesn't seem to be the problem.

1100711303.english: The atmosphere of the village itself seems exclusive.
1100711303.korean: 마을의 분위기 자체가 배타적인 거 같습니다.

1100711304.english: What will you do, Azazel-sama, Saraqael-sama?

1100711305.english: We are members of Orca and followers of Kohei.

1100711306.english: The common point between these two is that you should naturally extend a helping hand to those in need.
1100711306.korean: 이 두 가지의 공통점은 어려운 이가 있으면 마땅히 손을 내밀라는 겁니다.

1100711307.english: Not to invite you to join Orca, nor for the purpose of proselytizing.
1100711307.korean: 오르카에 합류를 권하기 위해서도, 포교를 위해서도 아닌.

1100711308.english: We just have to help because there is someone who needs it.
1100711308.korean: 단지 도움이 필요한 이가 있으니 도와야해요.

1100711309.english: Orca's greatest motto is helping those in need.
1100711309.korean: 오르카의 가장 큰 기치는 어려운 이를 돕는 거예요.

1100711310.english: And the Light said, "How can you be happy with the poor beside you? How can one be truly happy if he cannot picture the hungry at the table, the one shivering in the cold before the fire, or the homeless at his bed?"
1100711310.korean: 또한 빛께서 이르시되, 너희들은 어찌하여 불쌍한 이들을 옆에 두고 행복할 수 있느냐. 식탁 앞에서 굶주린 자의 모습을 떠올리지 못하고, 화롯불 앞에서 추위에 떠는 이의 모습을 떠올리지 못하고, 침대에서 집 없는 자의 모습을 떠올리지 못하는 자가 어찌 진실로 행복할 수 있겠느냐?

1100711312.english: Evangelism without dedication cannot reach people's hearts and cannot even follow one's own faith, so one cannot prioritize evangelism.
1100711312.korean: 헌신 없는 포교는 사람들의 마음에 닿지 못하고, 스스로의 신앙도 따르지 못하는 것이니 포교를 우선시 할 수는 없겠지.

1100711314.english: Then, what do you plan to do first?

1100711315.english: Hmm... shouldn't we fill people's stomachs first? If they are full, at least they will have fewer bad thoughts.
1100711315.korean: 음… 일단 사람들의 배부터 채워야하지 않을까요? 배가 부르면 적어도 나쁜 생각은 덜하게 되니까요.

1100711317.english: Then I will clean up the village. Wouldn't people feel motivated if they saw a clean village?
1100711317.korean: 그러면 저는 마을 청소를 할게요. 깨끗해진 마을을 보면 사람들도 의욕이 생기지 않겠어요?

1100711318.english: … … I will stay behind and help with the cleaning as well.

1100711319.english: Because I'll be better at cleaning than Azazel.
1100711319.korean: 아자젤 님보다는 제가 더 청소를 잘 할 테니까요.

1100711320.english: Hmm, next, what I can help with is… would you need medical assistance?
1100711320.korean: 음, 다음으로 도와드릴 것은… 의료지원이 필요할까요?

1100711321.english: I will get the medicine from Orca.

1100711322.english: Then, I will listen to the difficulties while talking to people.
1100711322.korean: 그러면 저는 사람들과 이야기를 하면서 고충을 들을게요.

1100711324.english: The Light said, "Knock until the door is opened, and speak until the heart is opened."
1100711324.korean: 빛께서 이르시되, 문은 열릴 때까지 두드리고 사람의 마음은 열릴 때까지 이야기를 하라.

1100711325.english: Even if it is cold right now, once they start talking, their hearts will gradually open up.
1100711325.korean: 지금 당장은 냉랭해도 한 번 말을 하기 시작하면 점차적으로 마음이 열리겠죠.

1100711326.english: It might take a long time.

1100711327.english: Yes. It might take a long time. They might not open their heart until the very end. But there is something to be gained, isn't there?
1100711327.korean: 네. 오래 걸릴 수도 있죠. 어쩌면 끝까지 마음을 열지 않을 수도 있고요. 하지만 얻는 것은 있잖아요.

1100711328.english: It makes things less difficult for people who are struggling.

1100711331.english: If you know that, please make things a little less difficult for me as well.
1100711331.korean: 그걸 아시면 저도 좀 덜 힘들게 해주십시오.

1100711334.english: Let's meet the villagers one by one and have a conversation with them.
1100711334.korean: 마을 사람 하나하나 만나면서 이야기를 나눠보도록 하죠.

1100711335.english: It would have been nice if Angel and Ramiel had come along too.
1100711335.korean: 엔젤과 라미엘도 함께 왔으면 좋았을 것을.

1100711336.english: But since both of you are busy, it’s hard to recommend.
1100711336.korean: 하지만 둘 다 바쁘니 권유하기 애매하군.

1100711338.english: Are Azazel and I the only ones who are less busy than usual?

1100711340.english: It must be just my imagination.

1100711344.english: Saraqael! You've come at just the right time!

1100711345.english: Iron Bugs have invaded the village!

1100711347.english: Let's find out the reason later!

1100711348.english: First, let's get rid of those Iron Bugs!

1100711349.english: Saraqael, Veronica. Are you hurt anywhere?

1100711350.english: This level of Iron Blooded is nothing. Azazel, what about you?
1100711350.korean: 이 정도 철충은 아무것도 아니지. 아자젤, 너는?

1100711352.korean: 아, 여러분. 혹시 다치신 분은 없으신가요?

1100711355.english: Is it because of you guys?

1100711357.english: Before you guys came, the Iron Blooded Beasts didn't attack!
1100711357.korean: 너희가 오기 전에는 철충이 쳐들어오지 않았어!

1100711358.english: The Iron Worms have passed by nearby before, but they've never attacked!
1100711358.korean: 철충이 근처를 지나간 적은 있어도 쳐들어오지는 않았단 말이야!

1100711359.english: Get out! Don't come back again!

1100711360.english: If it weren't for you, our village would be peaceful too!
1100711360.korean: 너희만 없으면 우리 마을도 평화로울 거라고!

1100711361.english: But, but it's not because of us-

1100711365.english: You don't know why the Iron Insect came here, do you?
1100711365.korean: 철충이 무슨 이유로 여기에 왔는지 모르지 않나.

1100711366.english: Whether it's because of us or for some other reason.
1100711366.korean: 우리 때문인지, 아니면 다른 이유가 있는지.

1100711367.english: If it's because of us, it's right to leave here.
1100711367.korean: 우리 때문이라면 여기를 떠나는 것이 옳다.

1100711371.english: Still... if you ever need our help, please don't hesitate to contact us.
1100711371.korean: 그래도… 만약에 저희 도움이 필요하시면 언제든지 연락주세요.

1100711375.english: There is a village even in a place like that.

1100711376.english: In my experience, it was strange that there wasn't a village in a place like this.
1100711376.korean: 내 경험상 이런 곳에 마을이 없는 것이 이상했어.

1100711377.english: A place with a water source, arable land, easy defense against attacks from other places, and a clear view of the surroundings.
1100711377.korean: 수원지가 있고, 농사 지을 땅이 있고, 다른 곳의 공격을 막기 쉽고, 주위가 잘 보이는 장소.

1100711378.english: And if you look closely, you can also find traces of old infrastructure remaining.
1100711378.korean: 그리고 잘 보면 옛날 인프라가 남아있는 흔적도 찾을 수 있고 말이야.

1100711379.english: It must be a village that hid from the eyes of the Iron Bug and Delta after the War of Annihilation.
1100711379.korean: 멸망 전쟁 이후에 철충과 델타의 눈을 피해서 숨은 마을이겠지.

1100711380.english: However, it is in the middle of the Iron Bug's territory.

1100711381.english: A battle will be inevitable to reach that village.
1100711381.korean: 저 마을에 도달하기 위해서는 전투는 불가피할 거예요.

1100711382.english: Haha, what are you worried about?

1100711383.english: If Iron Bugs block your path, just smash them all.
1100711383.korean: 철충이 앞을 가로막으면 전부 박살내면 그만이지.

1100711384.english: But Armand, what are you doing here?

1100711385.english: You're usually busy working as the commander's secretary.

1100711386.english: His Majesty said that those who can rest should rest for the time being.
1100711386.korean: 폐하께서 당분간은 쉴 수 있는 사람은 쉬라고 하셔서요.

1100711387.english: Then there's no need to come all the way here, right?

1100711388.english: This is work in its own way, too.

1100711390.english: I want to experience a different kind of fulfillment than when I was only by His Majesty's side.
1100711390.korean: 폐하의 곁에만 있을 때와는 다른 보람도 느껴보고 싶거든요.

1100711393.english: Just wrap up the conversation right there, ladies.

1100711394.english: The density of Iron Bugs has decreased. Now is the time to break through.
1100711394.korean: 철충의 밀도가 낮아졌어. 뚫고 가려면 지금이야.

1100711397.english: This is a very quaint village.

1100711399.english: I went to all that trouble to be indirect...

1100711401.english: Hello, miss! We are the Orca crew, led by the last human.
1100711401.korean: 안녕, 아가씨! 우리는 마지막 인간님이 이끄는 오르카의 대원들이야.

1100711402.english: Europe has become part of Orca's sphere of influence, so I'm going from village to village, resolving their problems and encouraging them to join us.
1100711402.korean: 유럽이 오르카의 세력권이 되어서 마을 하나하나를 돌면서 고충을 해결하고 합류를 권하는 중이야.

1100711404.english: How... did you manage to get here, past the Iron Beasts?

1100711405.english: That kind of thing is easy to break thru. Can you see my weapon? This one shot will solve everything.
1100711405.korean: 그 정도 쯤이야 가볍게 뚫고 들어오지. 내 무기 보이지? 이거 한 방이면 다 해결돼.

1100711408.english: Don't hesitate, just say it. If it's not possible, I'll tell you clearly that it's not.
1100711408.korean: 주저하지 말고 말해. 안 되는 거면 안 된다고 확실하게 말할 테니까.

1100711409.english: Excuse me... there's another village further in, isn't there?
1100711409.korean: 죄송한데… 저 안쪽에 마을 하나가 더 있거든요?

1100711410.english: Excuse me... could you also go to that village?
1100711410.korean: 죄송한데… 그 마을에도 가 주실 수 있나요?

1100711413.english: It'll probably be rougher than the way here.
1100711413.korean: 아마 여기까지 오시는 길보다 더 험하긴 할 텐데.

1100711414.english: You don't need to push yourself too hard...

1100711416.english: What's stopping it from working? Annie, Armand, can you go further?
1100711416.korean: 안 될 게 뭐야? 애니, 아르망 더 갈 수 있지?

1100711417.english: That's what a sheriff is for.

1100711418.english: If you were going to turn back here, you wouldn't have followed in the first place, would you?
1100711418.korean: 여기서 돌아갈 거면 처음부터 따라오지 않았겠죠?

1100711420.english: Um... could you give me a moment to write just one letter?
1100711420.korean: 그… 펴, 편지 한 장만 쓸 시간을 주시겠어요?

1100711421.english: If there's a Bioroid in that village who looks exactly like me... I mean, since we're the same model... it's my sister... Could you please tell that android...
1100711421.korean: 그 마을에 저랑 똑같이 생긴 바이오로이드가 있다면… 그러니까 저랑 같은 모델이니까… 제 동생인데... 그 바이오로이드에게 전해주실 수…

1100711422.english: Of course! One sheet? What are you talking about! Use ten or a hundred sheets, go ahead and use them!
1100711422.korean: 물론이지! 한 장이 뭐야! 열 장이고 백 장이고 팍팍 써와!

1100711423.english: Oh, no. I'm not that good at writing, so...
1100711423.korean: 아, 아뇨. 그 정도로 글 솜씨가 있는 건 아니라서…

1100711424.english: Oh, anyway, thank you! I'll write it and bring it back quickly!
1100711424.korean: 어, 어쨌든 감사합니다! 빨리 써 올게요!

1100711425.english: Now there is no road at all.

1100711426.english: How long have you been isolated?

1100711427.english: Even my Presley has reached its limit.

1100711428.english: There isn't enough space to pass, so moving forward is impossible…
1100711428.korean: 지나갈 공간이 부족하니 앞으로 나아가는 것도 불가능해…

1100711429.english: If there is no road, just make one!

1100711431.english: Hey, wait, miss! What are you going to do if the Iron Bugs swarm in while you're doing that!
1100711431.korean: 자, 잠깐, 아가씨! 그러다가 철충이 몰려오면 어쩌려고!

1100711432.english: Then the number of Iron Bugs in the world will decrease even more, right?

1100711433.english: … … I expected such an ignorant solution.

1100711434.korean: 추기경 아가씨! 저 아가씨 좀 말려봐!

1100711435.english: It’s okay. Because this is the best path right now.

1100711437.english: Look! The Iron Bugs are swarming in!

1100711438.english: Haha, what are you worried about?

1100711439.english: Alright, friends! Then let's make the world a little more peaceful!
1100711439.korean: 좋아, 친구들! 그러면 좀 더 세상을 평화롭게 만들어보자고!

1100711440.english: Oh, I thot I was going to die.

1100711441.english: You lived, didn't you? And reduce the number of Iron Blooded.

1100711442.english: After fighting in a place that completely killed Presley's mobility...
1100711442.korean: 프레슬리의 기동성을 다 죽이는 장소에서 전투를 해 놓고선…

1100711445.english: Oh? You found the person you were looking for right away, didn't you?

1100711447.english: Miss, do you have a sister?

1100711448.english: I, um, there is someone... but it's been a long time since we've seen each other.
1100711448.korean: 이, 있는데… 못 만난지 한참 됐어요.

1100711450.english: It's the same model as yours, isn't it?

1100711459.english: We broke up a long time ago... we couldn't meet... the path was cut off because of the Iron Plague... I thot we'd never see each other again until we died...
1100711459.korean: 옛날에 헤어지고… 못 만나서… 철충 때문에 길도 끊겨서… 죽을 때까지 못 볼 줄 알았는데…

1100711460.english: Hahaha... there are some misspelled words here too. I used to get this word wrong a lot back then too...
1100711460.korean: 하하하… 여기 단어 잘못 쓴 것도 있네요. 옛날에도 이 단어 자주 틀렸는데…

1100711461.english: Thank you... sob... thank you so much.

1100711462.english: As long as you're alive. If we're under the same sky, we'll meet someday.
1100711462.korean: 살아만 있으면. 한 하늘 아래에 있다면 언젠간 만날 수 있으니까.

1100711463.english: Okay, don't cry, smile. That's good news.
1100711463.korean: 자, 울지 말고 웃어. 기쁜 소식이잖아.

1100711468.english: Our goal is to move people's emotions.
1100711468.korean: 우리의 목적은 사람들의 감정을 움직이는 거죠.

1100711469.english: Our goal is to make people laugh, cry, get angry, and be terrified according to the scripts we create.
1100711469.korean: 만들어진 각본에 따라 사람들을 웃게 하고, 울게 하고, 화나게 하고, 겁에 질리게 하는 것이 우리들의 목적이죠.

1100711470.english: More real than real. This phrase is Dentsu's slogan, but...
1100711470.korean: 실제보다 더 실제같이. 이 말은 덴세츠의 구호지만…

1100711471.english: Chairman Yoshimitsu, the chairman of Densetsu, said that this statement reveals that what Densetsu does is ultimately fake.
1100711471.korean: 덴세츠의 회장이신 요시미츠 회장님은 이 말이 덴세츠가 하는 일은 결국엔 가짜라는 것을 드러내는 것이라고 하셨죠.

1100711472.english: What truly moves people is not something fake, but something real.
1100711472.korean: 사람을 정말로 감동시키는 것은 가짜가 아닌 진짜라고.

1100711473.english: No matter how hard our Legend's androids tried, they couldn't have given us greater joy than what that person is feeling right now.
1100711473.korean: 우리 덴세츠의 바이오로이드들이 아무리 노력해도 지금 저 분이 느끼는 기쁨보다 더 큰 기쁨을 줄 수는 없었을 거예요.

1100711474.english: Hmm... I have a slightly different opinion.

1100711476.english: Didn't we break thru the hordes of Iron Beasts, reach the previous village, receive the letter, break thru the hordes of Iron Beasts again, and then deliver the letter to this village?
1100711476.korean: 철충들의 무리를 뚫고 이전 마을에 도착해서 편지를 받고, 다시 철충 무리를 뚫은 후에 이 마을에 편지를 전해준 건 우리잖아?

1100711477.english: In the end, it was our effort that pleased that person, right?
1100711477.korean: 결국엔 우리가 노력해서 저 사람을 기쁘게 해준 거잖아.

1100711478.english: I also used to pursue virtual justice, but now you're really working for justice, aren't you?
1100711478.korean: 나 또한 가상의 정의를 추구했었지만 지금은 정말로 정의를 위해서 노력하잖아.

1100711479.english: Whether it's a legend or not doesn't matter.

1100711480.english: Life on stage is our life, but life off stage is also our life.
1100711480.korean: 무대 위의 삶도 우리의 삶이지만 무대 아래에서의 삶도 우리의 삶이니까.

1100711481.english: Your words feel somewhat off-topic from what I said, but...
1100711481.korean: 당신의 말은 제가 한 말의 논점에서 벗어난 느낌이 없잖아 있지만…

1100711483.english: So, do you feel fulfilled now too, miss? Didn't you say earlier that you wanted to feel a different sense of fulfillment than when you were only by the commander's side?
1100711483.korean: 그래서 아가씨도 지금 보람을 느껴? 아까 전에 사령관의 곁에만 있을 때와는 다른 보람도 느껴보고 싶다면서?

1100711484.english: It feels rewarding in a way that numbers and reports alone can't convey.
1100711484.korean: 숫자와 보고만으로는 알 수 없는 보람이 느껴지네요.

1100711485.english: And also the direction of what policies they will implement in the future.
1100711485.korean: 그리고 앞으로 어떤 정책을 펼칠지에 대한 방향성도요.

1100711486.english: This is Phantom. I have just returned from the IronBug's invisible zone.
1100711486.korean: 여기는 팬텀. 지금 막 철충의 불가시 영역으로부터 복귀했습니다.

1100711487.english: As a result of reconnaissance inside the invisible area, it was confirmed that Iron Bugs and Marionettes were mixed within the area.
1100711487.korean: 불가시 영역 내부 정찰 결과 영역에는 철충과 마리오네트가 혼성되어 있는 것을 확인했습니다.

1100711488.english: Marionette and Iron Bug are considered allies of each other.

1100711489.english: In particular, since the Marionette appears to have been produced after Delta's death, the hypothesis that the Iron Bug produces Marionettes seems confirmed.
1100711489.korean: 특히 마리오네트는 델타 사망 후에 생산된 것으로 보여, 철충이 마리오네트를 생산한다는 가설은 확정적인 것 같습니다.

1100711490.english: Deeper within the district, massive buildings of unknown purpose had been erected. However, their clear appearance could not be seen due to the station field.
1100711490.korean: 구역 더 깊은 곳에는 용도를 알 수 없는 거대한 건물들이 세워져 있었습니다. 하지만 역장으로 인해 명확한 모습은 볼 수 없었습니다.

1100711491.english: We attempted to approach the building to determine its true nature, but the infiltration failed due to the high density of Iron Bugs and Marionettes.
1100711491.korean: 건물의 정체를 파악하기 위해 접근을 시도해봤으나 철충과 마리오네트의 밀도가 높아 잠입은 실패했습니다.

1100711492.english: I request a diversionary operation for the infiltration.

1100711493.english: With this, I hope Alvis also understands Andvari's hard work.
1100711493.korean: 이걸로 알비스도 안드바리의 노고를 깨달았으면 좋겠네.

1100711495.english: Do you have anything else to do after this?

1100711497.english: I need to gain some more weight. It's so light, I can't feel anything.
1100711497.korean: 좀 더 살 찌워야겠어. 너무 가벼워서 아무런 느낌이 없네.

1100711499.english: I was just thinking about Leona.

1100711504.english: Have I become someone worthy of Leona?

1100711508.english: Do we need the power of Valhalla for this mission?

1100711509.english: Okay. I'll leave it to the dragon.

1100711512.english: I was disappointed because it was only on my cheek.

1100711519.english: What do you take me for?

1100711521.english: I like work too, but I like everyone at Orca even more.
1100711521.korean: 나는 일도 좋아하지만 오르카의 모두를 더 좋아하니까.

1100711522.english: Because the busier I am, the easier it is for everyone else.

1100711523.english: I can't just stand still when I think my work is for everyone.
1100711523.korean: 내 업무가 모두를 위한 것이라고 생각하면 가만히 있을 수가 없어.

1100711524.english: How can I possibly put down my work with this going on?
1100711524.korean: 이러니 내가 어떻게 일을 손에서 놓을 수 있겠어?

1100711525.english: ...and the work is so fun, I can't stand it.
1100711525.korean: ……그리고 일이 너무 재밌어서 버틸 수가 없어.

1100711526.english: Hahaha! No external pressure can stop me from doing my job!
1100711526.korean: 으하하하! 어떤 외압도 나의 업무를 막지 못한다!

1100711527.english: It feels like I've been on a long vacation.
1100711527.korean: 긴 휴가를 보낸 거 같은 기분이야.

1100711528.english: For me, working is like playing.

1100711531.english: Everyone's the main character, aren't they?

1100711532.english: It suits you well. Huge. It's very pretty. It's so lovely, my heart could burst.
1100711532.korean: 잘 어울려. 엄청. 무척 예뻐. 가슴이 터질 것처럼 사랑스러워.

1100711533.english: It's really great to meet everyone from Orca...

1100711534.english: I wouldn't be this happy if I hadn't met everyone.
1100711534.korean: 모두를 만나지 못했다면 이렇게 행복할 수 없었을 거야.

1100711535.english: When you purchase the next skin and gift it to the target combatant, the background will be activated.&nGriffon Dragon "Already 5 years"
1100711535.korean: 다음 스킨 구매 및 대상 전투원에게 선물 시, 배경이 활성화 됩니다.&n그리폰 용 "벌써 5년"

1100711536.english: When you purchase the next skin and gift it to the target combatant, the background will be activated.&nLocke the "Knight of the Skies Roc"
1100711536.korean: 다음 스킨 구매 및 대상 전투원에게 선물 시, 배경이 활성화 됩니다.&n로크 용 "창공의 기사 로크"

1100711537.english: When you purchase the next skin and gift it to the target combatant, the background will be activated.&n"The Nite Alone Came" for Lemonade Alpha
1100711537.korean: 다음 스킨 구매 및 대상 전투원에게 선물 시, 배경이 활성화 됩니다.&n레모네이드 알파 용 "어론 님 오신 밤"

1100711539.english: The background will be activated when purchasing the following skin and gifting it to the target combatant. "Silky on Vacation" for T-50 PX Silky
1100711539.korean: 다음 스킨 구매 및 대상 전투원에게 선물 시, 배경이 활성화 됩니다.&nT-50 PX 실키 용 "휴가지의 실키"

1100711540.english: When you purchase the next skin and gift it to the target combatant, the background will be activated.&n"Miss Orca: Self-Soothing and White Lies" for Lemonade Beta
1100711540.korean: 다음 스킨 구매 및 대상 전투원에게 선물 시, 배경이 활성화 됩니다.&n레모네이드 베타 용 "미스 오르카 : 자기 위로와 하얀 거짓말"

1100711541.english: When you purchase the next skin and gift it to the target combatant, the background will be activated.&nCheon-ah Dragon "Miss Orca: Regular Hot Pack Shopping"
1100711541.korean: 다음 스킨 구매 및 대상 전투원에게 선물 시, 배경이 활성화 됩니다.&n천아 용 "미스 오르카 : 정기 핫팩 쇼핑"

1100711542.english: When you purchase the next skin and gift it to the target combatant, the background will be activated.&nFafnir Dragon "Miss Orca: Lucky, Unlucky!" The Gambler's All-In Gambler Dress
1100711542.korean: 다음 스킨 구매 및 대상 전투원에게 선물 시, 배경이 활성화 됩니다.&n파프니르 용 "미스 오르카 : 럭키, 언럭키! 도박사의 올인 갬블러 드레스"
