1100711545.english: When you purchase the next skin and gift it to the target combatant, the background will be activated.&nAlcyone Dragon "Miss Orca: The Wave of Dreams"
1100711545.korean: 다음 스킨 구매 및 대상 전투원에게 선물 시, 배경이 활성화 됩니다.&n알키오네 용 "미스오르카 : 꿈꾸던 파랑(波浪)"

1100711548.english: The background will be activated when purchasing the following skin or gifting it to the target combatant. "Street Graffiti: Neon Techwear" for Ephnel.
1100711548.korean: 다음 스킨 구매 및 대상 전투원에게 선물 시, 배경이 활성화 됩니다.&n에프넬 용 "스트리트 그래피티 : 네온 테크웨어"

1200000003.english: How are you doing these days?

1200000005.english: How are you doing these days?

1200000009.english: Let's see, it looks like the wound in my mouth has healed...
1200000009.korean: 어디 보자, 입 안에 상처는 사라진 것 같은데…

1200000010.english: The other test results are also normal. It doesn't seem like the infection spread thru the respiratory system.
1200000010.korean: 다른 검사 결과도 정상이에요. 호흡기로 감염이 퍼지지는 않은 것 같아요.

1200000012.english: Yeah, they swallowed it just fine, no problem.

1200000013.english: Then... I guess the mouth ulcers are completely healed!
1200000013.korean: 그럼… 구내염은 다 나았다고 봐도 되겠네요!

1200000014.english: You've worked hard all this time, Baek-ah! CheonA, you've worked hard too.
1200000014.korean: 그 동안 고생 많았어, 백아야! 천아도 고생했고.

1200000015.english: Thank you. I was so worried when my healthy child suddenly started having trouble eating.
1200000015.korean: 고마워. 건강하던 애가 갑자기 밥도 잘 못 먹길래 걱정이 이만저만이 아니었는데.

1200000016.english: Honestly, I feed him well, but he still picks up strange things and ends up with cuts in his mouth...
1200000016.korean: 정말이지, 밥도 잘 주는데, 어디서 이상한 걸 주워 먹어서 입에 상처나 나고 말이야…

1200000017.english: You're too much trouble for a snake.

1200000018.english: Ahaha, don't be too harsh. It could be because Baek-ah is quite curious for a snake.
1200000018.korean: 아하하, 너무 구박하진 마. 백아는 뱀 치고는 호기심이 많은 편이라서 그럴 수도 있어.

1200000019.english: Since it's completely healed, it shouldn't be a big problem, but... is there anything else I should be careful about?
1200000019.korean: 다 나았으니까 크게 문제는 없을 것 같지만… 뭐 따로 조심해야 하는 건 없어?

1200000020.english: There's nothing you really need to be careful about!

1200000022.english: Well, that's just the way it is.

1200000023.english: Saying it like that sounds too insincere, Empress...
1200000023.korean: 그렇게 말하면 너무 성의 없게 들리잖아, 엠프리스...

1200000025.english: Snakes are actually quite delicate creatures, more so than they appear, and are very sensitive to ambient temperature and humidity. If the temperature or humidity isn't right, you can also get respiratory illnesses.
1200000025.korean: 뱀은 보기보다 굉장히 섬세한 동물이라서, 주변 온도나 습도에 굉장히 민감해요. 온도나 습도가 맞지 않으면 호흡기 질환에 걸리기도 하죠.

1200000026.english: However, there's no trace of respiratory illness found in Baek-ah. That means... Mr. Chun-ah must have taken really good care of it, right?
1200000026.korean: 하지만 백아는 호흡기 질환을 앓은 흔적을 찾아볼 수가 없어요. 그건… 그만큼 천아 씨가 관리를 잘 했다는 뜻이겠죠?

1200000027.english: Okay, so you're saying there's nothing to be careful about! Because you were already doing well.
1200000027.korean: 그래, 그래서 조심할 게 없다는 거야! 이미 잘 하고 있었으니까 말이야.

1200000029.english: What's there to be shy about? It's good to love the animals you raise, isn't it?
1200000029.korean: 쑥스러울 게 뭐 있어? 자기가 키우는 동물을 아낀다는 건 좋은 거잖아.

1200000030.english: That's true, but... it's the kind of thing that would get the hand warmers or JangHwa teasing me for days if they heard it.
1200000030.korean: 그렇기는 한데… 핫팩이나 장화가 들으면 며칠동안 놀릴 것 같은 말이라서.

1200000031.english: Anyway, that's a secret between those two. Got it?
1200000031.korean: 아무튼, 그건 그 두 사람한테는 비밀이야. 알겠지?

1200000032.english: Well... I don't quite understand, but okay.
1200000032.korean: 뭐… 이해가 잘 안 되기는 하지만, 알았어.

1200000033.english: It's not that hard to understand...

1200000034.english: Empress also says in front of others that she needs to raise Pengsoon strongly, but in reality, she usually indulges Pengsoon whenever she acts spoiled.
1200000034.korean: 엠프리스도 남들 앞에서는 펭순이를 강하게 키워야 한다고 말하지만, 사실 펭순이가 응석 부리는 건 어지간해서는 다 받아주잖아.

1200000035.english: Oh, um, how could you say that~!
1200000035.korean: 앗, 그, 그걸 얘기하면 어떻게 해~!

1200000036.english: ...Oh, this is what it feels like.

1200000038.english: Ugh, I don't think you needed to tell me like that...
1200000038.korean: 으으, 굳이 이렇게 알려줄 필요는 없던 것 같은데…

1200000039.english: As the saying goes, seeing is believing.

1200000040.english: ...Ella is really too much when you see her like this. Right, Pengsoon?
1200000040.korean: …엘라는 이럴 때 보면 진짜 너무하다니까. 그렇지, 펭순아?

1200000041.english: What? You think Ella is really mean too, right? I knew it, didn't I?
1200000041.korean: 뭐? 너도 엘라가 엄청 못됐다고 생각한다고? 역시 그렇지?

1200000043.english: So, is it okay for good me to secretly eat bad Ella's snacks...? Ow!
1200000043.korean: 그럼 착한 내가 못된 엘라의 간식을 몰래 먹어도 괜찮… 아야야야!

1200000045.english: I've been working out with the main Big Sis and Rena Big Sis lately, you know?
1200000045.korean: 나 요즘 메인 언니랑 레나 언니랑 같이 운동하거든?

1200000046.english: Don't pick it, don't break it! That joint isn't meant to bend that way!
1200000046.korean: 꺼, 꺾으면 안돼! 그 관절은 그쪽으로 돌아가도록 만들어져 있는 게 아니란 말이야!

1200000047.english: I'll stop when Pengsoon tells me to.
1200000047.korean: 펭순이가 그만 해도 된다고 말하면 멈춰줄게.

1200000053.english: ...Ahaha! Wow, the three of them are having a great time together.

1200000054.english: Anyway... seeing that, it makes me want to talk to Baek-ah too...
1200000054.korean: 그나저나… 저 모습을 보니까, 나도 백아랑 얘기를 해 보고 싶어지는데…

1200000055.english: Yeah, that would be difficult, wouldn't it? It's not like there's a machine that can translate animal speech.
1200000055.korean: 역시 그건 힘들겠지? 뭐 동물 말을 번역할 수 있는 기계가 있는 것도 아니고.

1200000056.english: Hmm, that's quite an interesting idea.

1200000057.english: ...Oh my goodness. I didn't sense any movement at all, where did you come from?
1200000057.korean: …깜짝이야. 기척을 전혀 못 느꼈는데, 어디서 나온 거야?

1200000058.english: Oh, did I startle you? I'm sorry. I was in the back room fixing Draculina's drone.
1200000058.korean: 아, 저 때문에 놀랐나요? 죄송해요. 드라큐리나의 드론을 고치느라 저 뒤쪽 방에 있었답니다.

1200000060.english: Yes. Some of the bat drones Draculina carries around are broken.
1200000060.korean: 네. 드라큐리나가 가지고 다니는 박쥐 드론 몇 개가 고장나서요.

1200000063.english: When people get hurt, they go to the hospital, and when animals get hurt, they go to the vet, right? If a machine gets damaged... you have to take it to a repair shop.
1200000063.korean: 사람이 다치면 병원에 가고, 동물이 다치면 동물 병원에 가잖아요? 기계가 다치면… 수리점에 가게 되고요.

1200000065.english: Then shouldn't the bat drones, which are machines that mimic animals, be repaired at the repair shop inside the animal hospital?
1200000065.korean: 그럼 동물을 모방한 기계인 박쥐 드론은 동물 병원 안쪽에 있는 수리점에서 고쳐야 하지 않을까요?

1200000070.english: Anyway, a machine that can translate animal speech... in other words, an animal language translator... that seems like a pretty interesting idea, doesn't it?
1200000070.korean: 아무튼, 동물의 말을 번역할 수 있는 기계… 즉 동물 언어 번역기라는 거, 꽤 재밌는 아이디어인 것 같은데…

1200000071.english: Would it be alright if I tried making it once?
1200000071.korean: 혹시 제가 한 번 만들어 봐도 괜찮을까요?

1200000072.english: Well... you don't really need to ask for permission. Beside, I didn't even patent that idea.
1200000072.korean: 뭐… 굳이 허락을 받을 필요는 없어. 내가 그 아이디어에 특허를 낸 것도 아니니까.

1200000073.english: And if something like that happens, it's good for me. Because I also want to talk to Baek-ah.
1200000073.korean: 그리고 그런 게 생기면 나야 좋지. 나도 백아랑 얘기를 해 보고 싶으니까.

1200000074.english: Then, could you help me with a task?
1200000074.korean: 그럼 작업을 하나 도와 주실 수 있나요?

1200000075.english: There's no reason it shouldn't work, but... I'm not sure if I'll be of any help? I don't have a PhD in engineering or anything like that.
1200000075.korean: 안 될 거야 없지만… 내가 도움이 될지는 모르겠네? 난 공학 박사 학위 같은 게 없어서.

1200000076.english: It's okay if you don't have a degree. It's about collecting voice data from animals.
1200000076.korean: 학위는 없어도 괜찮아요. 동물들의 음성 데이터를 수집하는 일이거든요.

1200000077.english: If we collect and organize animal vocalization data, wouldn't we be able to translate it into a language we can understand?
1200000077.korean: 동물들의 음성 데이터를 수집해서 체계화한다면 저희가 이해할 수 있는 언어로 변환할 수 있겠죠?

1200000078.english: ...It's hard to understand, but to summarize, all I need to do is bring back the recording, right?
1200000078.korean: …이해하기 힘든 말이지만, 정리하면 결국 녹음만 해 오면 되는 거지?

1200000079.english: Yes, that's right. I think about twenty samples will be enough.
1200000079.korean: 네, 그렇죠. 표본은 스무 개 정도만 있으면 될 것 같아요.

1200000081.english: Still, it's not that difficult. I'll handle this in a flash and be right back, so just wait!
1200000081.korean: 그래도 뭐, 어려울 거 없지. 순식간에 처리하고 올 테니까 기다리고 있어!

1200000082.english: At that time, Mr. CheonA didn't know yet.
1200000082.korean: 그 때, 천아 씨는 아직 모르고 있었던 거예요.

1200000083.english: Azaz is the type of person who, once they're focused on one thing, can't see anything else...
1200000083.korean: 아자즈는 하나에 몰입하면 다른 게 보이지 않는 성격이라는 것을…

1200000084.english: So, I was overlooking one... incredibly important fact.
1200000084.korean: 그래서, 한 가지… 엄청나게 중요한 사실을 간과하고 있었다는 것을.

1200000086.english: To be continued in the next story...

1200000087.english: No, who are you talking to?!

1200000089.english: I feel like I've lost my job for some reason.

1200000092.english: ...Anyway, for those reasons, I'd like to ask for some help.
1200000092.korean: …아무튼, 그런 이유로 도움을 좀 받고 싶은데.

1200000094.english: If you're asking me to bark like a dog, I...
1200000094.korean: 설마 멍멍 소리를 내달라고 부탁하려는 거라면…

1200000096.english: Thanks to you working in the city, you've got a lot of connections, haven't you? So, I was going to ask you to introduce me to some people who bring animals around.
1200000096.korean: 네가 신사에서 일한 덕분에 이래저래 발이 넓잖아? 그래서 동물들을 데리고 다니는 사람들을 좀 소개해달라고 하려고 했지.

1200000097.english: Oh, I see. I seem to have been mistaken for a moment.

1200000099.english: Our little puppy, are you still worried about barking when you were having a hot night with the hand warmer the other day?
1200000099.korean: 우리 똥강아지, 설마 저번에 핫팩이랑 뜨거~운 밤을 보낼 때 멍멍 소리 낸 걸 아직까지 신경 쓰고 있었어?

1200000100.english: That, that can't be true, can it? And I never barked! Don't spread strange rumors!!
1200000100.korean: 그, 그럴리가 없지 않나! 그리고 난 멍멍 소리도 낸 적 없다! 이상한 헛소문 내지 마라!!

1200000101.english: You know the video is on the Secret Network, right? Shall we see whose words are true?
1200000101.korean: 시크릿 네트워크에 영상 올라가 있는 거 알지? 어디 한 번 누구 말이 맞는지 검증해 볼까?

1200000105.english: The one who barked... it was me...
1200000105.korean: 멍멍 소리를 낸 건… 내가 맞다…

1200000106.english: Ahahaha! What's that expression? That's so cute!

1200000108.english: I'm not afraid of people who say they'll see later.
1200000108.korean: 나중에 두고 보자는 사람 안 무섭더라고.

1200000109.english: Anyway... now that I've said it, I am curious.

1200000111.english: I wonder what our Vargr's barking would translate to using an animal language translator. Maybe it'll be "Master, I love you so much~"?
1200000111.korean: 우리 바르그가 멍멍 소리 낸 걸 동물 언어 번역기로 번역하면 뭐라고 나올지. 아마도 ‘주인님 너무 좋아요~’가 되려나~?

1200000112.english: ...Ugh... there's no way it would translate! I'm not an animal.
1200000112.korean: …으윽… 번역이 될 리가 없지 않나! 난 동물이 아니니까.

1200000113.english: People are animals too, you know?

1200000114.english: No... can bio-roids not be considered animals? Where does a Bioroid fit in biological classification?
1200000114.korean: 아니다… 바이오로이드는 동물이라고 할 수 없나? 바이오로이드는 생물 분류로 어디에 속하지?

1200000115.english: Leaving that aside... it's true that I like my master, so I won't completely deny that statement.
1200000115.korean: 그건 차치하고… 주인님을 좋아하는 건 사실이니 그 말을 완전히 부정하진 않겠다.

1200000116.english: After all, a gentleman never breaks his word.
1200000116.korean: 군자는 식언을 하지 않는 법이니 말이다.

1200000118.english: It means you don't take back your word once you've said it.
1200000118.korean: 한번 뱉은 말은 다시 주워 삼키지 않는다는 말이다.

1200000119.english: Really? So, Mr. Vargr, what do you think about this statement?
1200000119.korean: 그래애? 그럼 이 말에 대해서는 어떻게 생각하시죠, 바르그 씨?

1200000121.english: I'll be honest. I have no intention of serving you as my master...
1200000121.korean: ‘솔직하게 말하지. 나는 너를 주인으로 모실 생각은…’

1200000123.english: ...You, you casually stab people where it hurts...
1200000123.korean: …네 녀석, 아무렇지 않게 사람의 아픈 곳을 찌르다니…

1200000124.english: Why do you say things you'll regret later?
1200000124.korean: 그러게 왜 나중에 후회할 말을 하고 그래?

1200000125.english: ...it was just the recklessness and foolishness of youth. If I had been more mature, and if I had known that you were this kind of person, I would never have said those words.
1200000125.korean: …어릴 때의 치기와 어리석음이었을 뿐이다. 내가 좀 더 성숙했다면, 또 주인님이 이런 분이라는 걸 알았다면 그런 말을 결코 내뱉지 않았을 거다.

1200000126.english: It doesn't seem like it was that long ago that you said that...
1200000126.korean: 그 말을 한 게 그렇게 오래 전의 일은 아니었던 것 같은데…

1200000127.english: ...Anyway, stop the teasing now. Didn't you come looking for me for a different reason?
1200000127.korean: …아무튼, 이제 희롱은 그만 둬라. 날 찾아온 이유는 따로 있지 않나?

1200000128.english: Oh, that's right, isn't it? Sorry, your reaction was so funny I completely forgot.
1200000128.korean: 참, 그랬지? 미안, 네 반응이 너무 웃겨서 까맣게 잊고 있었어.

1200000130.english: If you hit me, I might die, you know?

1200000133.english: Why are you so nervous? It's just a joke, don't worry.
1200000133.korean: 뭘 그렇게 긴장하고 그러나? 농담이니까 걱정하지 마라.

1200000134.english: ...You have a really dark sense of humor...

1200000135.english: Even if I'm going to dispose of you, shouldn't I at least finish cleaning the bathroom first? I don't know how long you plan to keep putting it off, but...
1200000135.korean: 널 처분하더라도 일단 화장실 청소는 끝내고 해야 하지 않겠나. 언제까지 미뤄둘 생각인지는 모르겠지만.

1200000136.english: No, the last Steel Line Fighter VI bet is invalid, I'm telling you! I was winning, but you suddenly unleashed killing intent, which messed up the manipulation and caused me to lose!
1200000136.korean: 아니, 저번 <스틸라인 파이터 VI> 내기는 무효라니까? 내가 이기고 있던 건데 네가 갑자기 살기를 뿜어내서 조작이 꼬인 탓에 진 거잖아!

1200000137.english: I'd like to take this opportunity to share a good quote that suits you well.
1200000137.korean: 이 기회에 네게 잘 어울리는 좋은 글귀를 하나 알려주고 싶군.

1200000139.english: A defeated general cannot be relied upon. It means the general of a defeated army cannot speak of valor.
1200000139.korean: 패군지장불가이언용. 패배한 군대의 장수는 무용을 말할 수 없다는 뜻이다.

1200000140.english: You lost the fight, so don't go around talking about this and that.
1200000140.korean: 싸움에서 진 주제에 이것저것 떠들지 말도록.

1200000141.english: Oh, do it again! Let's do it again!

1200000144.english: You're too weak. I don't compete against gamers who aren't skilled.
1200000144.korean: 네 녀석은 너무 약하니까. 난 실력이 없는 게이머와는 대결하지 않는다.

1200000145.english: ...you say I have no skill? You're Bronze! I'm Gold, you know?!

1200000146.english: A Gold tier player lost to a Bronze tier player, so wouldn't you say they're just plated?
1200000146.korean: 브론즈 티어에게 진 골드 티어니까… 도금이라고 해야 하지 않겠나?

1200000147.english: Ugh... I'll let it go because I think I'd lose if we fought, but you better be careful when you sleep...
1200000147.korean: 아오… 싸우면 질 것 같아서 넘어가겠는데, 너 진짜 잘 때 조심해라…

1200000148.english: I'm happy to accept any challenge like that.

1200000149.english: Anyway, going back to the story about the members who bring animals with them... did you go see Constantia?
1200000149.korean: 아무튼, 동물을 데리고 다니는 대원들 이야기로 돌아가서… 콘스탄챠에게는 찾아가 봤나?

1200000150.english: I've already been to see Constantia. Bori's voice recording is already finished.
1200000150.korean: 콘스탄챠한테는 이미 다녀왔지. 보리 목소리는 이미 녹음 끝났다고.

1200000151.english: Then, next would be the Saetti.

1200000152.english: Okay. I've always wanted to talk to Tiger at least once. And then Brunhilde...
1200000152.korean: 좋아. 나도 타이거랑 한 번쯤 말해 보고 싶긴 했지. 그리고 그 다음은 브륀힐드…

1200000153.english: Brunhilde's wolf is a robot, not a real animal or a bipedal android. Just be aware that it might not be suitable for recording animal sounds.
1200000153.korean: 브륀힐드의 늑대는 실제 동물도, 바이오로이드도 아닌 로봇이다. 동물 소리 녹음으로는 적합하지 않을 수도 있다는 걸 알아두도록.

1200000154.english: That's good information. Then it would be better to go find the Dark Elves.
1200000154.korean: 좋은 정보야. 그럼 다크엘븐을 찾아가는 편이 좋겠네.

1200000155.english: Next, it's a bit redundant, but I think I should go see Empress and Sleipnir, and finally, as expected...
1200000155.korean: 그 다음에는… 조금 겹치긴 하지만 엠프리스랑 슬레이프니르한테 찾아가면 될 것 같고, 마지막으로는 역시...
